claim: Mantan Presiden Rusia Dmitry Medvedev mengatakan pada Juni 2025 bahwa "sejumlah negara siap memasok langsung Iran dengan hulu ledak nuklir mereka sendiri."
evidence: Tentang peringkat ini Pada 22 Juni 2025, AS menyerang tiga fasilitas nuklir di Iran. Pada hari yang sama, unggahan di berbagai situs media sosial — termasuk Reddit (diarsipkan) dan X (diarsipkan) — mengklaim mantan Presiden Rusia Dmitry Medvedev secara publik memposting, "Sejumlah negara siap untuk memasok langsung Iran dengan hulu ledak nuklir mereka sendiri." Beberapa unggahan secara kolektif (diarsipkan, diarsipkan, diarsipkan) menerima jutaan tayangan. Komentar teratas menunjukkan beberapa pengguna percaya klaim itu palsu atau bahwa klaim spesifik tersebut tidak muncul dalam unggahan asli Medvedev. Terlepas dari keraguan para pengguna ini, Medvedev — yang menjabat sebagai wakil ketua Dewan Keamanan Rusia — memang secara publik menyatakan (diarsipkan) bahwa "sejumlah negara siap untuk memasok langsung Iran dengan hulu ledak nuklir mereka sendiri." Dalam serangkaian unggahan di akun X resmi, mantan presiden tersebut menulis pada 22 Juni: Beberapa pengguna di kolom komentar kemudian meminta Medvedev untuk mengklarifikasi apakah itu berarti Rusia akan memasok hulu ledak nuklir ke Iran, dan dalam pernyataan lanjutan (diarsipkan) lebih dari 24 jam kemudian, Medvedev mengatakan bahwa meskipun ia "mengutuk" serangan AS terhadap Iran, Rusia "tidak berniat memasok senjata nuklir ke Iran" mengingat Rusia adalah pihak dalam Perjanjian Non-Proliferasi Nuklir, yang juga diikuti oleh China dan Korea Utara. Dalam unggahan lanjutan ini pada 23 Juni, Medvedev juga menunjuk pada Perjanjian New START — sebuah perjanjian dari tahun 2011 antara AS dan Rusia yang menetapkan dan mempertahankan batasan pada senjata ofensif strategis — sebagai alasan yang lebih kuat mengapa "kita jelas tidak boleh berdebat tentang siapa yang memiliki lebih banyak senjata nuklir." Pada 23 Juni, Presiden AS Donald Trump menanggapi pernyataan Medvedev di platform media sosialnya, Truth Social (diarsipkan), bahwa banyak negara siap memasok senjata nuklir ke Iran. "Kata 'N' [nuklir] tidak boleh diperlakukan begitu saja," tulisnya, sebelum kemudian memuji pasukan AS dalam operasi 22 Juni melawan situs nuklir Iran. (Pengguna Truth Social @realDonaldTrump) Wakil Presiden AS JD Vance juga menanggapi dalam sebuah wawancara dengan ABC News pada 23 Juni, mengatakan klaim Medvedev "aneh" tetapi dia tidak tahu apakah Medvedev berbicara atas nama Putin atau pemerintah Rusia (pada 4:15).

claim: Posts authentically showed a letter that U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi sent to FBI Director Kash Patel about the Epstein files in February 2025.
evidence: About this rating Following a July 2025 memo from the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI that said convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein did not keep a "client list" — contrary to a persistent rumor — some social media users shared a screenshot of a purported letter that they suggested contradicted the official finding. U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi sent the alleged letter dated Feb. 27, 2025, to FBI Director Kash Patel, according to the social media posts. In the letter, Bondi "acknowledged receiving the Epstein files containing" the convicted sex offender's "list of contacts and a list of victims' names," a Facebook user who shared the purported letter claimed. Snopes readers emailed us and searched our website to ask if the letter from Bondi to Patel was authentic. Bondi did send this letter about the Epstein files to Patel, DOJ records show. Therefore, we've rated the claim true. A copy of the letter was attached to a DOJ news release dated Feb. 27, 2025, in which the agency announced Bondi, in conjunction with the FBI, declassified and released files related to Epstein. Snopes compared the version of the letter shared on social media to the letter shared on the DOJ website, and found that the text in both versions was identical. The files released in February 2025 didn't include any new revelations about the Epstein case since they had long been publicly available through court cases and media reports. The DOJ acknowledged this in its news release, saying the released Epstein files contained documents that were "previously leaked but never released in a formal capacity" by the federal government. They included an evidence list, flight logs, a redacted contact book and a redacted masseuse list. Bondi received approximately 200 pages of documents in response to a request for all of the files related to Epstein, according to the release. However, she was "later informed of thousands of pages of documents related to the investigation and indictment that were not previously disclosed," the release said. In her letter to Patel, Bondi addressed the alleged missing documents and asked the FBI to deliver "the full and complete Epstein files" to her office by 8 a.m. on Feb. 28, 2025. Bondi also tasked Patel with investigating why the FBI did not follow her order requesting the full set of Epstein files. "You will deliver to me a comprehensive report of your findings and proposed personnel action within 14 days," Bondi wrote in the letter. The letter from Bondi to Patel never explicitly mentioned Epstein's "client list," though it did say she received "Epstein's list of contacts" in response to her request for the Epstein files. In the first paragraph of her letter, Bondi wrote (emphasis ours): Before you came into office, I requested the full and complete files related to Jeffrey Epstein. In response to this request, I received approximately 200 pages of documents, which consisted primarily of flight logs, Epstein's list of contacts, and a list of victims' names and phone numbers. A redacted contact book was shared on the DOJ website. Additionally, in her request for the FBI to deliver the "full and complete Epstein files" to her office, Bondi said such a delivery would include "all records, documents, audio and video recordings, and materials related to Jeffrey Epstein and his clients, regardless of how such information was obtained" (emphasis ours). The full letter from Bondi to Patel is as follows: Dear Director Patel, Before you came into office, I requested the full and complete files related to Jeffrey Epstein. In response to this request, I received approximately 200 pages of documents, which consisted primarily of flight logs, Epstein's list of contacts, and a list of victims' names and phone numbers. I repeatedly questioned whether this was the full set of documents responsive to my request and was repeatedly assured by the FBI that we had received the full set of documents. Late yesterday, I learned from a source that the FBI Field Office in New York was in possession of thousands of pages of documents related to the investigation and indictment of Epstein. Despite my repeated requests, the FBI never disclosed the existence of these files. When you and I spoke yesterday, you were just as surprised as I was to learn this new information. By 8:00 a.m. tomorrow, February 28, the FBI will deliver the full and complete Epstein files to my office, including all records, documents, audio and video recordings, and materials related to Jeffrey Epstein and his clients, regardless of how such information was obtained. There will be no withholdings or limitations to my or your access. The Department of Justice will ensure that any public disclosure of these files will be done in a manner to protect the privacy of victims and in accordance with law, as I have done my entire career as a prosecutor. I am also directing you to conduct an immediate investigation into why my order to the FBI was not followed. You will deliver to me a comprehensive report of your findings and proposed personnel action within 14 days. I appreciate our immediate attention to this important matter. I know that we are both committed to transparency for the American people and I look forward to continuing to work with you to serve our President and our country. Sincerely, Pamela Bondi Attorney General (U.S. Department of Justice) Despite claims to the contrary, the DOJ and FBI said in a July 2025 memo that Epstein did not keep a "client list" and the agencies confirmed no additional files related to the investigation would be made public. A review of "investigative holdings" related to Epstein "revealed no incriminating 'client list,'" the DOJ and FBI wrote in the memo. The agencies continued: There was also no credible evidence found that Epstein blackmailed prominent individuals as part of his actions. We did not uncover evidence that could predicate an investigation against uncharged third parties. According to the memo, the DOJ and FBI also determined that "no further disclosure" of information related to the Epstein investigation "would be appropriate or warranted." With its assertion that Epstein did not have a "client list," the Trump administration backpedaled on a theory it had previously helped promote, as The Associated Press reported. Attorney General Pam Bondi told Fox News in February 2025 that the Epstein client list was "sitting on my desk right now to review," citing a "directive" from Trump. Snopes reached out to the DOJ for comment about online users' suggestions that the Feb. 27 letter from Bondi to Patel proved the existence of a "client list" tied to Epstein, and we will update this story if we receive a response. The Trump administration has faced criticism from Republicans and Democrats alike over its handling of the Epstein files. In response, the president lashed out at his critics, including some of his supporters, in a lengthy Truth Social post shared on July 16, 2025. He wrote, in part: Their new SCAM is what we will forever call the Jeffrey Epstein Hoax, and my PAST supporters have bought into this "bull----," hook, line, and sinker. They haven't learned their lesson, and probably never will, even after being conned by the Lunatic Left for 8 long years. I have had more success in 6 months than perhaps any President in our Country's history, and all these people want to talk about, with strong prodding by the Fake News and the success starved Dems, is the Jeffrey Epstein Hoax. Let these weaklings continue forward and do the Democrats work, don't even think about talking of our incredible and unprecedented success, because I don't want their support anymore! For further reading, Snopes investigated if a photo showed Trump, Epstein and Bondi together, and whether Ghislaine Maxwell, an associate of Epstein, offered to testify before Congress about the Epstein files.

claim: Elon Musk posted a message on X that read, "Without me, Trump would have lost the election, Dems would control the House and the Republicans would be 51-49 in the Senate."
evidence: About this rating Amid rising tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk, a former special government employee, posts on X on June 5, 2025, claimed to show a screenshot from Musk's official account in which he took credit for Trump's 2024 reelection. "Without me, Trump would have lost the election, Dems would control the House and the Republicans would be 51-49 in the Senate," Musk allegedly wrote in the post. One version of the claim shared by @PopBase (archived) quickly garnered more than 2.1 million views and 5,000 shares. Elon Musk says Trump would have lost the election without him:"Without me, Trump would have lost the election, Dems would control the House and the Republicans would be 51-49 in the Senate. […] Such ingratitude" pic.twitter.com/Gw6FVqm7YM — Pop Base (@PopBase) June 5, 2025 Snopes searched Musk's verified X account and found the following post (archived): (X user @elonmusk) He followed up with a reply (archived) that read, "Such ingratitude." Because Musk posted the words in the screenshot, the claim is correctly attributed. Musk was replying to a post from an X user (archived), who shared a video of Trump speaking about Musk's recent opposition to the GOP's "Big Beautiful Bill." 🚨 NEW: Trump speaks on Elon coming out against the BBB"I would have won Pennsylvania regardless of Elon…I'm very disappointed with Elon. He knew this bill better than anyone and he only developed a problem when he found out I would cut the EV mandate…When he left he said… pic.twitter.com/O0OfkqmOUd — Autism Capital 🧩 (@AutismCapital) June 5, 2025 In the video, Trump suggested he would have won Pennsylvania even if Musk had not campaigned for his reelection in the swing state and expressed his disappointment in Musk's criticism of the bill. "I'm very disappointed because Elon knew the inner workings of this bill better than almost anybody sitting here, better than you people. He knew everything about it, he had no problem with it. All of a sudden he had a problem with it and he only developed the problem when he found out that we're going to have to cut the EV mandate because that's billions and billions of dollars," Trump said in the video. Musk's reply to the post quickly reignited claims that Musk helped Trump "rig" the 2024 presidential election. Snopes previously fact-checked claims that Musk's Starlink satellite network was used to tally swing-state votes during the 2024 election, allowing him to "rig" or "hack" it in favor of Trump, and rated those claims as unfounded. In response to Musk's post, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told Snopes via an emailed statement: "This is an unfortunate episode from Elon, who is unhappy with the One Big Beautiful Bill because it does not include the policies he wanted. The President is focused on passing this historic piece of legislation and making our country great again." Also on June 5, Musk posted a poll on X asking whether there should be a new political party in the U.S. to represent the "80% in the middle." The billionaire also alleged on the same day that Trump appears in unreleased government files related to deceased convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein."

claim: Indian Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi admitted that six jets were lost, and 250 soldiers were martyred in the recent conflict with Pakistan.
evidence: A video claiming to show Chief of the Army Staff (COAS) General Upendra Dwivedi confirming that six Indian jets were lost, and 250 soldiers were martyred in the May 2025 military confrontation with Pakistan has gone viral online.  In the video, General Dwivedi is purportedly heard saying "…the next war which we are looking at, it may happen soon and we have to prepare accordingly…This non-kinetic has to be fought together. We have already lost six jets and 250 soldiers but we will never give up. We will learn. We have some deductions. So what are the deductions?…"  Newschecker fact checked the video and found it to be manipulated.  Multiple Pakistan-based X handles, including verified profiles, shared the viral with the caption, "Indian Army Chief admits losing 6 jets and 250 soldiers in war against Pakistan, says preparing for the next war against Pakistan soon."  Screengrab from X post by @Jan_Achakzai    Such posts can be seen here, here and here.  Also Read: Fake Amul Ad Mocking Trump Tariffs Shared As Real  Fact Check/Verification  A keyword search for "Army Chief" and "six jets lost" on Google did not yield any credible report/ official release carrying the alleged statement by General Upendra Dwivedi.    We then looked up the keyframes of the viral clip on Google Lens which directed us to a post on the official YouTube channel of the Indian Army, dated August 9, 2025. It features the Army Chief's address at IIT Madras, showing him in the same setting background as in the viral footage.  Around 11 minutes into the video, we heard the COAS saying, "…the non-kinetic quadrant is something which you can start today and the next war which we are looking at, it may happen soon and we have to prepare accordingly, as already brought out by director that in this we have to fight this battle together. This non-kinetic has to be fought together. So what are the deductions. That as far as whole of nation approach will be there for the next war it is not the military alone which will be fighting…"  Screengrab from YouTube video by @ADGPIINDIANARMY      The segment did not mention anything about the alleged loss to India during conflict with Pakistan, contradicting the viral video.  Also Read: Trump Tariff – India Cut Down Generic Drugs Export To US By Half? Here's The Truth Behind 'News Report By Rajat Sharma'  An excerpt from his speech was shared by ANI News, whose watermark is the viral clip, on YouTube on August 11, 2025, stating, "During an address at IIT Madras, Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Upendra Dwivedi in a cryptic message hinted that India is preparing for 'next war' and it may 'happen soon'."  At the 1:28 minute mark, we heard the Army Chief making the above mentioned remarks. This clip too, did not contain any information about the alleged loss of men and machinery during recent clashes with Pakistan, confirming that a fake audio has been interested into the COAS' speech to propagate a false narrative.  AI Alterations Detected On carefully analysing the viral clip, we observed a change in audio between 18-24 seconds in the clip, coinciding with the alleged remark. General Dwivedi's jaw movement appeared unnatural too, hinting towards AI manipulation. We then looked up the section on AI detection website Hive Moderation which concluded an overall 99.9% likelihood of the media containing AI generated or deepfake content. Screengrab from Hive Moderation website Another AI content detection tool Deepware also found "deepfake" in the viral clip. Newschecker also ran past the audio on Hiya Deepfake Voice Detector, which found it to be "likely deepfake." (L-R) Screengrabs from Deepware website and Hiya Deepfake Voice Detector tool No Such Remarks By Army Chief, Clarifies PIB The fact checking unit of government's PIB also flagged the viral clip, alleging to show General Dwivedi admitting that India lost 6 jets and 250 soldiers in the war against Pakistan, as "AI generated/deepfake," and asserted that the COAS has not made any such statement. Also Read: Did Indian Air Force Chief Admit To Losing 6 Jets In Conflict With Pakistan? No, Viral Clip Is Manipulated Conclusion Hence, the viral video, allegedly showing the Indian Army Chief admitting to the loss of jets and personnel during conflict with Pakistan, was found to be altered.  Sources  YouTube Video By @ADGPIINDIANARMY, Dated August 9, 2025  YouTube Video By ANI News, Dated August 11, 2025  Hive Moderation Website  Deepware Website  Hiya Deepfake Voice Detector Tool  X Post By PIB Fact Check, Dated August 12, 2025

claim: Conservative commentator Charlie Kirk once said that if he had a 10-year-old daughter who became pregnant after being raped, he would want her to deliver the baby.
evidence: About this rating The question was a hypothetical, although Kirk did have an infant daughter at the time. After conservative pundit Charlie Kirk was fatally shot Sept. 10, 2025, during an event at Utah Valley University, social media users sharing numerous posts containing statements Kirk had supposedly made. One such claim, shared on multiple platforms, was that Kirk once said that if he had 10-year-old daughter who became pregnant after being raped, he would want her to deliver the baby. Snopes readers searched the site looking for more information about the purported statement. A post shared by Feminist News (@feminist_news_now) We found the statement was real. It came from a 2024 YouTube video published by Jubilee titled "Can 25 Liberal College Students Outsmart 1 Conservative?" The video was the first in a series called "Surrounded," where one person must defend and debate their beliefs to a group of 20-plus people who hold opposing beliefs. (Critics have said the format prioritizes sound bites over logical arguments and that it provides a platform for extremists). Kirk defended four claims in the video, the first of which was "Abortion is murder and should be illegal." The group surrounding Kirk was given 20 minutes to debate him on the topic. With three minutes remaining, a woman identified as Maren had the following exchange with Kirk. The transcript has been edited for clarity because both Kirk and Maren talked over each other at points: MAREN: So I just have one question, there's — in any case, you don't think there's any case where abortion should be legal? KIRK: There is a very, very rare couple cases — MAREN: OK, so you do think there are a couple cases it is legal? KIRK: If cesarean section is not going to save the mother's life, and the mother's life is actually at risk, which is debated amongst growing numbers of OB-GYNs, that is the only case where abortion should be allowed. But, it is a growing consensus in the pro-life world that abortion is never medically necessary. MAREN: OK. So if you had a daughter and she was 10 and she got raped, and — KIRK: Well, I do have a daughter. MAREN: Wait, and she was going to give birth and she was going to live, would you want her to go through that and carry her rapist's baby? KIRK: Well, that's awfully graphic. MAREN: No, but it's a real-life scenario that happens to many people. KIRK: Calm down, the answer is yes, the baby would be delivered. MAREN: Oh, OK great, that's insane. KIRK: But let me tell you why. No, hold on, let me ask you a question. There's two ultrasounds — one is a baby conceived in rape, one is a baby conceived by a loving couple. Which one is which? [points around the room] MAREN: Your daughter was hopefully conceived by a somewhat loving couple. KIRK: Which person here was conceived by rape? Tell me which one was conceived by rape? MAREN: You don't know. KIRK: Exactly, it's all human rights and it's all human beings. MAREN: But that doesn't matter, but it's about your daughter who has to give birth to it and is gonna be tortured by that for the rest of her life. That's gonna take away every freedom she's ever gonna have, that's gonna ruin her life, she's gonna grow up and she's gonna be attached to another thing. KIRK: Abortion is not a victimless crime. The point is, how you were conceived is irrelevant to what human rights you get. If a person conceived in rape walks down the side of the street, it's not like they don't get First Amendment rights or Second Amendment rights. MAREN: It's not about that person — KIRK: The worst thing to do to that daughter is to then say, "Hey, we're going to go murder the being inside of you." MAREN: They wouldn't even know — like, listen. KIRK: They wouldn't know? MAREN: Listen, listen, listen. KIRK: But wouldn't it be a better story to say something evil happened and we do something good in the face of evil instead of saying we're going to do evil and then murder the being because we're gonna pander to the evil? No. What makes the West great is that we do good after evil, not evil after evil.

claim: Fact Check: WHO has not approved home remedy for COVID-19, don’t fall for misinformation
evidence: As much as we would like to believe that something like this is true, till date, no cure or treatment, especially home remedy has been found to be effective in rooting out the virus. Neither have they been approved by WHO or any such health body. The claim talks about a concoction made out of simple kitchen ingredients like turmeric, ginger and honey which when taken for five days can crush the viral spread. While all of these may work as excellent virus fighters (and help fight a bad bout of cold), there is no truth to the claim that this home remedy can cure COVID-19.

claim: Govt of India is providing money to bereaved families of COVID-19 - Insurance benefits for Covid-19 deaths can be claimed under the schemes of PMJJBY and PMSBY.
evidence: A message is being shared widely on social media with a claim that the benefits under the Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) and Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) can be claimed for COVID-19 related deaths. Claim: The benefits under the PMJJBY and PMSBY schemes can be claimed for COVID-19 related deaths. Fact: While it is true that the PMJJBY scheme is applicable to COVID-19 deaths, there are some conditions with respect to the eligibility and claim process. The PMSBY scheme caters only to accidents and as of today, there is no update regarding the inclusion of COVID-19 related cases in PMSBY.

claim: In November 2025, a town in Montana that voted overwhelmingly for U.S. President Donald Trump held a "pedophile bonfire" to burn MAGA merchandise.
evidence: About this rating As the U.S. House of Representatives prepared to vote to release files related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein in November 2025, a rumor spread online that a Montana town that voted overwhelmingly for President Donald Trump was holding a "pedophile bonfire" to burn Trump flags and hats that said "Make America Great Again," the slogan that started the MAGA movement. The claim circulated after the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform released a batch of more than 20,000 documents related to the Epstein case, including many emails to and from Epstein that mentioned Trump. For example, one Facebook page posted the rumor on Nov. 14 (archived), generating more than 23,000 shares as of this writing. The post read: BREAKING: A Montana town that voted 89% for Donald Trump a year ago is holding a "Pedohile Bonfire" event in their public park tonight for anyone who wants to come burn their Trump flags and MAGA hats. Variations of the rumor spread elsewhere on Facebook (including in Spanish) and on X and Instagram. None of the examples listed specified which town the bonfire was supposed to take place in. Some people seemed to believe the event was real, and several Snopes readers emailed us to ask whether it was true. Online searches uncovered no evidence that such an event was set to take place, or had taken place, in Montana in November 2025. Rather, the rumor about the MAGA merch bonfire originated with The Halfway Post (archived, archived) — a website and series of social media accounts describing its output as satirical. Its About page read (archived): I'm Dash MacIntyre, a comedian, satirist, and poet from St. Louis. I founded The Halfway Post to make fun of Donald Trump. I don't report the facts, I improve them because comedy is cathartic in fascist eras. Its article about the fabricated bonfire in a Montana town also included clear satire labels. The Halfway Post has a history of writing and fabricating satirical stories. In this case, it appeared to draw inspiration from some incidents that reportedly occurred in July 2025, during which Trump supporters were said to have burned MAGA merch to express their anger over the way Trump was handling the issue of the Epstein files. Meanwhile, other rumors circulated that people were burning their MAGA hats after the November 2025 Epstein documents release. Snopes had not verified whether these claims were true or where they allegedly occurred. Snopes has previously addressed similar satirical claims from The Halfway Post, including the assertion that Republican legislators had tapes of Trump begging, bribing, threatening and crying over them voting to release the Epstein files. For background, here is why we alert readers to rumors created by sources that call their output humorous or satirical.

claim: In early September 2025, U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Labor Day should be a celebration of "women who go through labor."
evidence: About this rating In early September 2025, a claim circulated on social media that U.S. Vice President JD Vance suggested Labor Day should be a celebration of "women who go through labor." One post (archived) on Threads spread the rumor with an image that read: WASHINGTON-JD Vance stirred controversy on Monday by suggesting that Labor Day be a celebration of "women who go through labor." "The woke mob has distorted the true meaning of Labor Day," Vance told Fox News "Honoring workers might please people who work for a living, but it doesn't delight me and Donald Trump." Vance urged that Labor Day be "a time to pay tribute to the one thing that gives women's lives meaning: reproducing." "Only women who have given birth should be allowed at Labor Day picnics," he declared. "No baby, no hot dog." (Threads user @the_bad_girl_from_the_tree) The user added the caption, "No baby, no hot dog," referring to the final section of the text. The rumor also spread on Facebook, Threads, X and Instagram. Some readers seemed to interpret the rumor as a factual recounting of real-life events and turned to Snopes' website to verify whether it was true. However, there was no evidence to substantiate the rumor that Vance shared this sentiment. A Google search revealed no reports from reputable news organizations about the alleged statement. Given the prominence of a U.S. vice president, major media outlets would have widely reported this statement. Rather, the story was created by Andy Borowitz, a comedian and satirist known for creating the satirical publication The Borowitz Report, which he's been publishing online since 2001. The site's about page reads, in part: I've been writing satirical news since I was eighteen. This represents either commitment to a genre or arrested development. In high school, I became editor of the newspaper solely because it produced an annual April Fool's issue. Later, as president of The Harvard Lampoon, I published parodies of the college newspaper, which got me hauled into the office of Dean Archie C. Epps III, which was his actual name. For the next two decades, I took a break from news satire while I waited for the Internet to be invented. Then, in 2001, I started emailing made-up news stories to friends. One suggested that creating a "website" would make it easier to "blast" my "posts." Soon, The Borowitz Report was live at BorowitzReport.com, and my free newsletter was reaching untold dozens of people. In a Sept. 1, 2025, Facebook post (archived) that originated the rumor, Borowitz wrote: WASHINGTON—JD Vance stirred controversy on Monday by suggesting that Labor Day be a celebration of "women who go through labor." "The woke mob has distorted the true meaning of Labor Day," Vance told Fox News "Honoring workers might please people who work for a living, but it doesn't delight me and Donald Trump." Vance urged that Labor Day be "a time to pay tribute to the one thing that gives women's lives meaning: reproducing." "Only women who have given birth should be allowed at Labor Day picnics," he declared. "No baby, no hot dog." The image featured in the post was from a January 2025 Fox News interview with Vance. At no point in that interview did Vance mention Labor Day. The Borowitz Report published a nearly identical story titled "JD Vance Says Labor Day Should Celebrate Women who go Through Labor" in September 2024, which likely served as the basis for the 2025 version. It started: LANSING (The Borowitz Report)—JD Vance stirred controversy on Monday by suggesting that Labor Day be a celebration of "women who go through labor." "The woke mob has distorted the true meaning of Labor Day," Vance told supporters in Michigan. "Honoring workers might please people who work for a living, but it doesn't delight me [...] The full version of that article was available only to paid subscribers. We investigated similar rumors involving Vance in the past, including a claim that he said women in violent marriages should not get divorced and a rumor he once agreed with a podcast host who claimed having grandmothers help raise children is "the whole purpose of the postmenopausal female." Snopes also has addressed multiple satirical claims from The Borowitz Report in the past. For background, here is why we alert readers to rumors created by sources that call their output humorous or satirical.

claim: Trump Administration claimed songwriter Billie Eilish Is Destroying Our Country In Leaked Documents
evidence: A Washington Post story wrongly claimed the Trump administration accused Billie Eilish of “destroying our country” — then spread like wildfire among the entertainment industry. The article on Thursday — in part about hundreds of celebrities being vetted for a Trump administration coronavirus ad campaign — included a misattributed quote from the White House about Eilish. “Donald Trump is destroying our country and everything we care about,” the Grammy-winning singer said at the Democratic National Convention in August. Citing leaked documents obtained by Democratic House lawmakers, the Washington Post incorrectly wrote that the Trump officials described Eilish as “not a Trump supporter,” saying she is “destroying our country and everything we care about.”

claim: In 2013, then-businessman and reality TV host Donald Trump said: "A government shutdown falls on the president's lack of leadership. I mean, problems start from the top and they have to get solved from the top. A shutdown means the president is weak."
evidence: About this rating Trump truly once said the exact words in the second sentence of the quote. No record existed of Trump saying the exact words in the first or third sentences. However, his other remarks from that period of time aligned with the same thoughts. Amid the U.S. government shutdown that started on Oct. 1, 2025, a rumor circulated online that, in 2013, then-businessman and reality TV host Donald Trumpsaid: "A government shutdown falls on the president's lack of leadership. I mean, problems start from the top and they have to get solved from the top. A shutdown means the president is weak." According to memes and posts featuring the quote, Trump — who was president during the 2025 shutdown — issued these remarks days before a government shutdown during then-President Barack Obama's second term in October 2013. On Sept. 30, the anti-Trump (archived) Occupy Democrats Facebook page posted a meme (archived) featuring the quote. The group shared the meme elsewhere on X and Instagram, with captions reading: "This Trump quote aged beautifully… and for once, we agree with him!" Snopes readers also searched for the claim on our site. (Occupy Democrats/Facebook) Many other users shared the same memes or similar ones, or simply reposted the quote's text, on Bluesky, Facebook, Instagram (archived), Threads (archived) and X (archived). Searches of Bing, DuckDuckGo, Google and Yahoo uncovered no record of Trump producing the verbatim remark about government shutdowns. Only the second sentence reflected an exact quote. However, looking at Trump's comments from 2013 —which he made on TV and on X (then Twitter) — revealed that the remainder of the alleged quote was a fair paraphrase of his publicly stated thoughts. Therefore, the quote in the memes is a mixture of true and false information. Those same online searches located one of the earliest-known posts (archived) containing the first and third sentences of the purported quote from Jan. 20, 2018, during another government shutdown that occurred during Trump's first term —indicating those words originated around the same time. A White House spokesperson responded to our email about the quote but did not answer our questions. We also contacted Occupy Democrats by email to ask about their post and their alleged exact quote and will update this article if we receive further information. On Sept. 30, 2013, Trump appeared on the Fox News morning TV show "Fox & Friends." At the time, Obama was serving as president, Republicans controlled the House and Democrats held the majority in the Senate. For years, a 29-second clip from the interview spread online: During the discussion, "Fox & Friends" co-host Elisabeth Hasselbeck asked Trump, "Who's getting fired, who's going to bear the brunt of the responsibility if indeed there is a shutdown of our government?" He replied by mentioning the importance of leading and exhibiting "strength" — the opposite of weakness (the in-question quote featured the word "weak"): It always has to be the top. I mean, problems start from the top and they have to get solved from the top. And the president's the leader and he's got to get everybody in a room and he's got to lead. And he doesn't do that. He doesn't like doing that. That's not his strength. As shown, he also produced the exact words from the second sentence of the in-question quote: "I mean, problems start from the top and they have to get solved from the top." Another version of the clip appeared on the Internet Archive's TV News Archive website. In the longer video of the interview, Trump said a compromise to avoid a shutdown in 2013 would "look like a failure to the Democrats," who, again, controlled the White House and the Senate at the time. In the October 2025 shutdown, the Republicans controlled the White House, Senate and the House. Trump and the White House repeatedly blamed the 2025 shutdown on the Democrats (archived, archived, archived). Trump also said in the 2013 interview that he believed people in future decades and centuries, during their discussions about past government shutdowns, would remember only the presidential administration in power at the timeand would not recall leaders in Congress: You know, the interesting thing is, in 25 years, in 50 years and 100 years from now, when the government is, you know when they talk about the government shutdown, they're going to be talking about the president of the United States, "Who was the president at that time?" They're not going to be talking who the head of the House was, the head of the Senate, who's running things in Washington. They're going to be talking about, "During the Obama administration, it was a disaster." On April 7, 2011 —one day before elected officials struck an eleventh-hour budget deal to head off another potential government shutdown (archived, archived) — NBC's "Today" show aired a segment featuring co-host Meredith Vieira interviewing Trump. NBC recorded the interview on April 6. During the video-recorded interview, Trump said of government shutdowns: "It's not really shutting down the government. It's cutting costs. The kind of money that you're talking about is peanuts." He then said he thought "the president [Obama at the time] would be blamed," adding: "If there is a shutdown, I think it would be a tremendously negative mark on the president of the United States. He's the one that has to get people together." Vieira then asked Trump how he would respond if he were in the Oval Office. He responded: "I would get everybody together and we'd have a budget." Vieira replied, referencing Obama and congressional leaders: "They've all gotten together." Trump retorted: "Well, that's because they don't have the right leader. You don't have the right leader." Ahead of the 2013 shutdown, Trump tweeted a message blaming it on Obama (archived), writing: "FACT – the reason why Americans have to worry about a government shutdown is because Obama refuses to pass a budget." On Oct. 1, the first day of the 2013 shutdown, Trump responded (archived), "True!" to a user who said "if you were in office this would of never happen." The next day, Trump posted (archived): "Stocks rose yesterday during the first day of government shutdown. Markets like being left alone for a day." Weeks later, on Nov. 8, Trump wrote (archived): "Leadership: Whatever happens, you're responsible. If it doesn't happen, you're responsible." Trump also previously posted the word "weak" in reference to both Obama and, generally, leadership in Washington. In sum, although no evidence was found of Trump saying, "A government shutdown falls on the president's lack of leadership. I mean, problems start from the top and they have to get solved from the top. A shutdown means the president is weak," in its entirety, there was proof he produced the second sentence verbatim. Meanwhile, the rest of the quote, though not said word for word, did fairly reflect other comments he made in 2013. For further reading, we previously reported on a different claim regarding a similar rumor about Trump truly blaming Obama for the 2013 shutdown. That article featured several additional Trump remarks made on the Fox News TV show, "On the Record w/ Greta Van Susteren."

claim: "Hanya dalam 12 tahun sejak lahirnya desentralisasi, kami telah mentransfer total KSh4 triliun ke berbagai daerah."
evidence: Ruto benar bahwa pemerintah daerah akan menerima KSh415 miliar pada tahun anggaran 2025/26, hampir KSh30 miliar lebih banyak dari tahun sebelumnya. Angka KSh4 triliun yang ditransfer ke pemerintah daerah sejak desentralisasi dimulai juga terbukti benar. Namun, presiden melebih-lebihkan jumlah orang yang terdaftar di eCitizen, platform layanan digital pemerintah, dan klaimnya tentang miliaran yang dikumpulkan melalui platform tersebut tidak dapat diverifikasi. Ketua Dewan Gubernur Ahmed Abdullahi akurat bahwa alokasi untuk pemerintah daerah berjumlah antara 10 dan 13% dari anggaran nasional. Pada Agustus 2025, Presiden William Ruto bergabung dengan 47 gubernur Kenya di Kabupaten Homa Bay, sekitar 400 kilometer barat daya Nairobi, untuk konferensi desentralisasi tahunan. Kini memasuki tahun ke-12, forum ini telah dikritik oleh beberapa pihak sebagai "pemborosan waktu dan sumber daya" tetapi sering dilihat sebagai momen penting bagi sistem desentralisasi negara, yang didirikan pada tahun 2010, untuk mengevaluasi kemajuan dan kekurangannya. Ruto menyebutkan triliunan shilling yang dikirim ke daerah-daerah dan miliaran yang mengalir melalui eCitizen, gerbang digital pemerintah, sebagai bukti dukungan pemerintah nasional yang sering dikritik terhadap desentralisasi. Ketua Dewan Gubernur Ahmed Abdullahi lebih lanjut menyoroti keberhasilan di tingkat daerah, dengan menyebutkan lebih banyak fasilitas kesehatan dan perekrutan dokter dan perawat tambahan. Tetapi apakah klaim kedua pemimpin tersebut sesuai? Kami memeriksanya. Presiden William Ruto mengatakan angka-angka tersebut menunjukkan komitmen pemerintahannya untuk mendelegasikan kekuasaan dan sumber daya kepada 47 daerah di Kenya. Konstitusi menjamin daerah-daerah setidaknya 15% dari pendapatan nasional yang diaudit terbaru, yang dikenal sebagai bagian yang adil. Daerah-daerah juga mendapatkan hibah dari pemerintah nasional. Di bawah desentralisasi, parlemen, yang terdiri dari majelis nasional dan senat, mengalokasikan dana tersebut. Perbendaharaan negara melepaskan uang tersebut, dan pengawas anggaran mengotorisasi setiap transfer. Alokasi untuk daerah-daerah ditetapkan setiap tahun dalam undang-undang pembagian pendapatan. Data terbaru, untuk tahun 2025, menunjukkan bahwa kabupaten-kabupaten menerima KSh415 miliar (sekitar US$3,2 miliar). Pada tahun 2024, angkanya adalah KSh400 miliar, tetapi dipotong di pertengahan tahun menjadi KSh387,43 miliar setelah protes anti-pajak yang dipimpin oleh kaum muda. Jumlah yang lebih rendah itu dikonfirmasi oleh pengawas anggaran dan pernyataan pendapatan aktual serta pelepasan kas negara pada Juli 2025 dari Departemen Keuangan. Ruto benar dalam hal ini. Bagian yang adil untuk kabupaten-kabupaten pada tahun 2025 adalah KSh415 miliar. Pada tahun 2024, jumlahnya adalah KSh387,43 miliar. Itu berarti tambahan KSh27,57 miliar – hampir KSh30 miliar lebih banyak. Africa Check juga mengumpulkan data historis, menunjukkan bahwa alokasi tahun 2025 adalah yang tertinggi hingga saat ini yang diterima oleh kabupaten-kabupaten. Ruto mengatakan pemerintah nasional telah mentransfer KSh4 triliun (sekitar $30,74 miliar) ke kabupaten-kabupaten selama masa jabatan mereka. Data Departemen Keuangan menunjukkan bahwa sejak implementasi pemerintahan daerah setelah pemilihan umum Maret 2013 hingga Juni 2024, daerah-daerah menerima KSh3,6 triliun. Antara Juli 2024 dan Juni 2025, mereka menerima tambahan KSh418,3 miliar, diikuti oleh KSh229,2 miliar pada Juli 2025. Pada saat presiden menyampaikan klaimnya, total transfer mencapai KSh4,24 triliun. Ruto benar. Ruto menyoroti pertumbuhan eCitizen, platform digital Kenya untuk mengakses dan membayar layanan pemerintah, mengajukan dokumen, dan memeriksa catatan resmi. Ia mengatakan platform tersebut menawarkan lebih dari 22.000 layanan pemerintah, meningkat dari 300 layanan pada saat peluncurannya. Data tersebut mendukung pertumbuhan yang signifikan: Pada Juni 2023, Ruto meluncurkan penambahan 5.084 layanan baru, yang dikonfirmasi dalam laporan sektoral Departemen Keuangan November 2023. Pada Oktober 2024, Kementerian Keuangan melaporkan 16.000 layanan. Pada bulan Desember, direktorat eCitizen menyebutkan angka tersebut menjadi 22.515. Laporan auditor jenderal Maret 2025 mencatat bahwa 222 entitas dan lebih dari 22.000 layanan telah tersedia di platform tersebut. Dalam pidato anggaran Juni 2025, Menteri Keuangan John Mbadi mengatakan kepada parlemen bahwa jumlah layanan yang didigitalisasi telah meningkat "dari 350 pada tahun 2022 menjadi 20.985 pada tahun 2025". Pada bulan yang sama, direktorat eCitizen menerbitkan buletin yang menyebutkan 21.000 layanan yang telah sepenuhnya terintegrasi, dengan layanan lainnya masih dalam proses integrasi. Banner halaman utama resmi eCitizen pada saat publikasi berbunyi: "Lebih dari 22.000 layanan Pemerintah kini tersedia dari 100+ Kementerian, Kabupaten, Departemen, dan Lembaga." Bukti menunjukkan ekspansi yang stabil, dengan jumlah layanan yang sedikit berbeda di berbagai sumber resmi. Catatan yang tersedia untuk umum memang mendukung angka 22.000 yang disebutkan Ruto, meskipun tidak ada penghitungan pasti. Ruto mengatakan bahwa "lebih dari 14 juta warga Kenya" telah mendaftar di platform eCitizen. Angka yang tersedia menunjukkan cerita yang berbeda. Pada Desember 2024, direktorat eCitizen melaporkan 13 juta pengguna terdaftar. Pada Juni 2025, direktur jenderal Isaac Ochieng mengatakan platform tersebut memiliki 13,5 juta pengguna pada akhir tahun fiskal 2024/25. Pada 16 Agustus, Kenya News Agency, layanan pers milik pemerintah, melaporkan bahwa sekretaris utama imigrasi dan layanan warga negara, Belio Kipsang, menyebutkan angka 13,67 juta. Data yang tersedia untuk umum tidak mendukung klaim Ruto tentang "lebih" dari 14 juta. Dia mungkin telah membulatkan angka tersebut, tetapi mengatakan "lebih" berarti kita menilai klaim ini sebagai berlebihan. Ruto mengatakan uang yang dikumpulkan melalui platform eCitizen telah meningkat dari "60 juta KSh menjadi lebih dari 1 miliar KSh" setiap hari. Laporan audit pendapatan eCitizen oleh auditor jenderal untuk tahun keuangan 2021/22 menunjukkan bahwa mereka mengumpulkan 21,2 miliar KSh, atau rata-rata 58 juta KSh per hari. Pada Juni 2023, angka ini meningkat menjadi 26,4 miliar KSh atau sekitar 72,3 juta KSh setiap hari. Laporan Maret 2025 oleh auditor jenderal menunjukkan bahwa pada Juni 2024, pengumpulan telah mencapai 100,8 miliar KSh, setara dengan 276 juta KSh per hari. Ini merupakan peningkatan 282% antara peluncuran digitalisasi oleh Ruto dan tahun 2024, bukan 1.500% atau lebih. Presiden juga mengatakan eCitizen menghasilkan "lebih dari 500 miliar KSh [3,84 miliar dolar AS] per tahun". Pada 16 Agustus 2025, sekretaris utama imigrasi dan layanan warga negara Belio Kipsang dilaporkan mengatakan bahwa total penerimaan "sejak awal" pada tahun 2013 adalah KSh500 miliar. Ia menyebutkan rata-rata penerimaan harian antara KSh700 juta dan KSh1 miliar. Untuk mencapai angka Ruto, mereka perlu rata-rata setidaknya KSh1,37 miliar per hari. Tidak ada data yang tersedia untuk umum yang mengkonfirmasi hal ini atau lonjakan 1.500% yang diklaim Ruto. Angka Juni 2025 belum keluar pada saat publikasi. Karena hampir semua layanan pemerintah sekarang ada di eCitizen, kami tidak dapat memberikan penilaian tanpa data tersebut. Ahmed Abdullahi, ketua dewan gubernur, memuji desentralisasi karena meningkatkan infrastruktur kesehatan, mengklaim bahwa fasilitas publik sekarang mencakup "lebih dari 45% dari semua fasilitas kesehatan di negara ini". Bukti menunjukkan sebaliknya. Sensus Kementerian Kesehatan yang diterbitkan pada September 2023 menemukan bahwa 47% fasilitas kesehatan adalah milik publik, 46% swasta, dan 8% berbasis agama atau dikelola oleh organisasi non-pemerintah (LSM). Kantor statistik negara tersebut menerbitkan data tentang fasilitas kesehatan operasional berdasarkan kepemilikan dalam survei ekonomi tahunannya. Survei tahun 2025 secara sementara mencatat 7.002 fasilitas publik dari total 15.984 pada tahun 2024, atau sekitar 44%. Sisanya adalah 47% swasta, 7% berbasis agama, dan 2% dikelola oleh LSM. Angka "lebih dari 45%" yang disebutkan Abdullahi tidak didukung oleh data terbaru. Data menunjukkan 43,8%, atau 44% jika dibulatkan. Memang mendekati, tetapi kata "lebih dari" sekali lagi melebih-lebihkan kebenaran. Abdullahi mengatakan bahwa kabupaten hanya menerima 10 hingga 13% dari anggaran tahunan Kenya, dengan sebagian besar uang, 87 hingga 90%, ditahan oleh pemerintah nasional. Kabupaten-kabupaten dialokasikan dana melalui anggaran nasional, bukan diberikan kendali langsung atasnya, kata pakar devolusi Dr. Conrad Bosire kepada Africa Check. Ia telah banyak menulis tentang topik ini di Kenya. "'Mendevolusikan anggaran' secara harfiah dapat berarti mentransfer kekuasaan dan proses pembuatan anggaran dari pemerintah nasional ke 47 kabupaten, yang tidak praktis. Perbedaan ini penting, bukan sekadar basa-basi," kata Bosire. Abdullahi tampaknya merujuk pada alokasi kabupaten relatif terhadap total anggaran nasional. Untuk tahun 2025/26, undang-undang pembagian pendapatan menetapkan alokasi kabupaten sebesar KSh415 miliar dibandingkan dengan anggaran nasional sebesar KSh4,24 triliun. Itu berarti 9,8%, mendekati angka terendah yang disebutkan Abdullahi. "Ya, kita dapat mengatakan bahwa 87 hingga 90% anggaran 'berada di pusat' meskipun kabupaten memiliki kewenangan untuk mengumpulkan pendapatan karena kabupaten, berdasarkan rancangan konstitusional, tidak mampu mengumpulkan sebagian besar sumber daya yang dibutuhkan untuk mendanai fungsi mereka," kata Bosire, menambahkan bahwa hal ini membuat mereka sengaja bergantung pada pendapatan yang dikumpulkan secara nasional untuk operasional mereka. Ia membagikan laporan resmi yang menunjukkan bahwa pemerintah pusat telah mempertahankan beberapa fungsi yang ditugaskan kepada kabupaten, beserta dana yang terkait dengannya. Bosire juga mencatat bahwa sejak tahun 2013, negara telah banyak meminjam untuk proyek-proyek modal, dengan pembayaran dipotong terlebih dahulu dari dana konsolidasi sebelum alokasi untuk kabupaten dilakukan. Setiap peningkatan biaya ini mengurangi uang yang tersedia untuk kabupaten. Klaim tersebut dapat diterima. Kami menilai sebagian besar klaim tersebut benar.

claim: Pusat penahanan migran yang dikenal sebagai "Alligator Alcatraz" diperkirakan akan dibuka di Florida pada tahun 2025.
evidence: Tentang peringkat ini Di tengah penindakan imigrasi yang dipimpin oleh pemerintahan Presiden AS Donald Trump, unggahan yang beredar online pada akhir Juni 2025 mengklaim bahwa pusat penahanan migran yang dikenal sebagai "Alligator Alcatraz" akan segera dibuka di Florida. Misalnya, seorang pengguna X membagikan unggahan berikut: 🚨 BERITA TERBARU: Presiden Trump kemungkinan akan menghadiri pembukaan besar ALLIGATOR ALCATRAZ di Florida pada hari Selasa, menurut Palm Beach Post. Ini akan LUAR BIASA 🔥 Ribuan imigran ilegal ditempatkan di tengah-tengah buaya dan ular piton, tepat di sebelah bandara tempat mereka dapat diterbangkan… pic.twitter.com/SbLcRQH5aT — Eric Daugherty (@EricLDaugh) 29 Juni 2025 Klaim serupa beredar di Facebook. Sebuah akun bernama "Donald Trump for President" membagikan pesan ini pada 29 Juni 2025: Pembaca Snopes juga mengirim email kepada kami dan mencari di situs web kami untuk menanyakan apakah "Alligator Alcatraz" itu nyata. Pusat penahanan sementara baru yang dikenal sebagai "Alligator Alcatraz" resmi dibuka di Florida pada 1 Juli 2025, menurut pejabat federal dan Jaksa Agung Florida James Uthmeier. Oleh karena itu, kami menilai klaim ini benar. Seperti yang dilaporkan Snopes sebelumnya, Uthmeier mengusulkan pusat penahanan untuk migran yang dikenal sebagai "Alligator Alcatraz." Dia memposting video yang merinci rencana untuk fasilitas tersebut di akun X dan Facebook terverifikasinya pada 19 Juni 2025, dan sebelumnya membahas proposal tersebut selama segmen dengan "Fox Business." Pada saat itu, Uthmeier menggambarkan "Alligator Alcatraz" sebagai "pusat layanan terpadu untuk melaksanakan agenda deportasi massal Presiden Trump." Dalam video yang dibagikannya di media sosial, dia mengatakan, sebagian: Florida telah memimpin dalam penegakan hukum imigrasi, mendukung upaya pemerintahan Trump dan ICE untuk menahan dan mendeportasi imigran ilegal yang melakukan tindak kriminal. Gubernur menugaskan para pemimpin negara bagian untuk mengidentifikasi tempat-tempat untuk fasilitas penahanan sementara baru. Saya pikir ini yang terbaik, seperti yang saya sebut, Alligator Alcatraz. Area seluas 30 mil persegi ini sepenuhnya dikelilingi oleh Everglades [dan] menghadirkan peluang yang efisien dan berbiaya rendah untuk membangun fasilitas penahanan sementara karena Anda tidak perlu berinvestasi banyak di perimeter. Orang-orang keluar, tidak banyak yang menunggu mereka selain buaya dan ular piton. Tidak ada tempat untuk pergi, tidak ada tempat untuk bersembunyi. Pemerintah federal mengkonfirmasi beberapa hari kemudian bahwa mereka bermitra dengan Florida dalam proyek "Alligator Alcatraz." Dalam sebuah unggahan X yang dibagikan pada 23 Juni 2025, Departemen Keamanan Dalam Negeri (DHS) menulis: Kami sedang berupaya mencari cara yang hemat biaya dan inovatif untuk memenuhi mandat rakyat Amerika untuk deportasi massal. Alligator Alcatraz akan memperluas fasilitas dan kapasitas tempat tidur hanya dalam beberapa hari, berkat kemitraan kami dengan Florida. Beberapa hari sebelum pembukaan "Alligator Alcatraz," Partai Republik Florida juga mengiklankan barang dagangan resmi yang tersedia untuk dibeli secara online. Sekretaris Pers Gedung Putih Karoline Leavitt mengumumkan bahwa Trump dan Menteri Keamanan Dalam Negeri Kristi Noem akan melakukan perjalanan ke Florida pada 1 Juli 2025, untuk pembukaan pusat penahanan migran baru di Bandara Pelatihan dan Transisi Dade-Collier. Fasilitas yang terletak "di jantung Everglades" itu akan "secara informal dikenal sebagai Alligator Alcatraz," kata Leavitt. Uthmeier juga mengumumkan pembukaan "Alligator Alcatraz" dalam sebuah unggahan pada 1 Juli 2025. "Fasilitas yang dioperasikan negara bagian" yang terletak di Ochopee, Florida, "diperkirakan memiliki hingga 3.000 tempat tidur," kata Badan Imigrasi dan Bea Cukai AS (ICE) dalam sebuah unggahan. Florida berencana untuk menampung 2.000 orang lagi di pusat penahanan terpisah di fasilitas Garda Nasional yang disebut Camp Blanding, kata Gubernur negara bagian Ron DeSantis selama kunjungan ke "Alligator Alcatraz." Pembangunan fasilitas tersebut diperkirakan akan dimulai "tepat setelah" Hari Kemerdekaan, menurut Direktur Eksekutif Divisi Manajemen Darurat Florida, Kevin Guthrie. Selama kunjungan tersebut, Noem mendorong negara bagian lain untuk mengeksplorasi opsi serupa. Dia mengatakan, sebagian: Florida unik dalam apa yang mereka presentasikan kepada kami dan saya akan meminta setiap gubernur lain untuk melakukan hal yang sama persis. Ini unik karena kita dapat menahan individu di sini. Mereka dapat menjalani sidang untuk mendapatkan proses hukum yang adil dan segera diterbangkan kembali ke negara asal mereka. Sebelum pembukaan "Alligator Alcatraz," Noem juga mengatakan kepada CBS News bahwa fasilitas penahanan di Florida akan didanai "sebagian besar" oleh Program Penampungan dan Layanan Badan Manajemen Darurat Federal (FEMA), sebuah inisiatif yang dibuat oleh Kongres yang "memberikan dukungan keuangan kepada entitas non-federal" untuk menawarkan penampungan dan layanan terkait kepada migran yang dibebaskan dari tahanan DHS. Pusat penahanan "Alligator Alcatraz" diperkirakan akan menelan biaya $450 juta per tahun, kata seorang pejabat AS kepada Associated Press. Arsip Snopes turut berkontribusi dalam laporan ini.

Gambar demonstrasi Kamboja secara keliru dikaitkan dengan konflik Thailand

Diterbitkan pada 6 Agustus 2025 pukul 10:18
Diperbarui pada 6 Agustus 2025 pukul 10:21

2 menit baca

Oleh

Chayanit ITTHIPONGMAETEE,

AFP Thailand

Bangkok telah mendesak Phnom Penh untuk mengambil jenazah tentaranya yang tewas selama lima hari bentrokan antara kedua negara tetangga di Asia Tenggara tersebut, tetapi sebuah gambar yang diunggah di media sosial tidak menunjukkan para demonstran Kamboja mengajukan tuntutan yang sama kepada para pemimpin mereka. Gambar tersebut diambil dua minggu sebelum konflik dan menggambarkan para pedagang pasar yang memprotes tindakan keras pemerintah provinsi terhadap kios mereka.

"Rakyat Khmer memberontak!! Protes berlanjut," demikian sebagian teks berbahasa Thailand yang superimposed pada gambar yang telah dilihat lebih dari 40.000 kali di TikTok sejak diunggah pada 3 Agustus 2025. "Rakyat Kamboja mendesak Hun Sen untuk mencari tentara garis depan yang tidak dapat dihubungi. Gugur untuk negara tetapi ditinggalkan sebagai mayat tanpa keluarga." Hun Sen adalah mantan pemimpin Kamboja, yang tetap memiliki peran berpengaruh dalam pemerintahan. Putranya, Hun Manet, telah memimpin Kamboja sejak 2023. Gambar tersebut menunjukkan sekelompok orang yang memegang foto dan tanda.

Gambar

Tangkapan layar unggahan TikTok palsu yang diambil pada 4 Agustus 2025, dengan tanda X merah ditambahkan oleh AFP

Gambar yang sama juga beredar di unggahan TikTok serupa. Gambar tersebut muncul setelah Thailand dan Kamboja menyepakati gencatan senjata pada 29 Juli, setelah lima hari bentrokan yang menewaskan sedikitnya 43 orang di kedua pihak -- letusan terbaru dari sengketa perbatasan yang telah berlangsung lama (diarsipkan di sini). Konflik tersebut juga menyebabkan lebih dari 300.000 orang mengungsi dari rumah mereka, dan memicu serangan disinformasi karena pendukung Thailand dan Kamboja sama-sama berusaha untuk meningkatkan narasi bahwa pihak lainlah yang harus disalahkan (tautan arsip). Bangkok Post melaporkan bahwa Wakil Menteri Pertahanan Thailand Nattapon Narkphanit telah meminta Kamboja untuk mengambil jenazah tentara mereka yang gugur yang tertinggal di perbatasan -- menambahkan bahwa penundaan apa pun akan "merendahkan kehormatan dan martabat mereka" serta menimbulkan risiko kesehatan bagi desa-desa terdekat (tautan arsip). Namun, gambar yang beredar tidak menunjukkan para pengunjuk rasa Kamboja yang juga menuntut pemerintah mereka untuk mengambil jenazah tersebut. Pedagang pasar Pencarian gambar terbalik di Google mengarah ke rekaman yang diunggah di YouTube pada 11 Juli -- dua minggu sebelum bentrokan perbatasan terbaru (tautan arsip). Gambar yang dibagikan secara salah tersebut tampaknya merupakan cuplikan dari rekaman ini. Keterangan berbahasa Thailand berbunyi: "Siaran langsung dari pasar Kamboja. Para pedagang memprotes dengan agresif, meminta Hun Sen untuk membantu insiden di Pasar Chhnuk Trou… Kampong Chhnang pada 11 Juli 2025."

Gambar

Perbandingan tangkapan layar gambar yang dibagikan secara salah (kiri) dan klip YouTube yang dibagikan pada 11 Juli (kanan)

Pencarian kata kunci lebih lanjut mengarah ke klip serupa yang dibagikan di halaman Facebook situs berita Kamboja CJ News pada 11 Juli (tautan arsip) "Laporan Jurnalis Warga: Para pedagang di Pasar Chhnuk Trou meminta bantuan Hun Manet karena pihak berwenang Kampong Chhnang membongkar paksa kios mereka," demikian bunyi unggahan berbahasa Khmer tersebut.

Gambar

Perbandingan tangkapan layar gambar yang dibagikan secara salah (kiri) dan unggahan CJ News (kanan), dengan elemen-elemen terkait yang disorot oleh AFP

AFP juga telah membantah informasi yang salah lainnya terkait konflik antara Kamboja dan Thailand. 6 Agustus 2025 Judul dikoreksi menjadi "gambar" alih-alih "klip"

AFP belum memperoleh hak apa pun dari penulis atau pemilik hak cipta konten pihak ketiga ini dan tidak akan bertanggung jawab dalam hal ini. AFP dan logonya adalah merek dagang terdaftar.

claim: Mantan Ketua DPR dari Partai Republik, Newt Gingrich, membagikan sebuah unggahan yang mendukung penggunaan ledakan nuklir untuk membuat kanal yang akan berfungsi sebagai jalur pelayaran alternatif ke Selat Hormuz.
evidence: Tentang penilaian ini: Proposal yang dibagikan Gingrich dalam unggahannya berasal dari artikel satir. Kami tidak dapat memastikan apakah Gingrich menanggapi artikel tersebut dengan serius ketika ia membagikannya. Pada Maret 2026, mantan Ketua DPR dari Partai Republik, Newt Gingrich, tampaknya menganjurkan penggunaan ledakan nuklir untuk menciptakan kanal baru sebagai alternatif Selat Hormuz yang dikendalikan Iran. Tangkapan layar dari unggahan X yang diduga membagikan sentimen ini menyebar secara online. Unggahan di atas dari akun X resmi Gingrich adalah nyata; oleh karena itu, kami menilai klaim ini sebagai benar. Namun, artikel yang ia bagikan bersifat satir, dan tidak jelas apakah Gingrich menanggapi artikel tersebut dengan serius. Kami telah menghubungi Gingrich untuk mempelajari lebih lanjut dan akan memperbarui unggahan ini sesuai dengan informasi yang kami terima. Pada 15 Maret, akun X terverifikasi Gingrich memposting (diarsipkan) kutipan berikut dari sebuah artikel Substack: "Daripada terus-menerus memperebutkan jalur sempit selebar 21 mil, kita membuat saluran baru melalui wilayah yang bersahabat. Selusin ledakan termonuklir dan Anda akan mendapatkan jalur air yang lebih lebar dari Terusan Panama, lebih dalam dari Terusan Suez, dan aman dari serangan Iran." (Pengguna X @NewtGingrich) Postingan asli, "Saatnya Tiba," muncul di halaman Substack berjudul ChinaTalk. Substack menggambarkan dirinya sebagai "lembaga pemikir dan media hibrida nonpartisan dengan keahlian mendalam tentang Tiongkok dan teknologi yang sedang berkembang." Bagian akhir postingan blog menyatakan: "Pandangan yang diungkapkan di atas belum tentu mewakili pandangan siapa pun yang memiliki akal sehat." Ini menunjukkan bahwa postingan tersebut ditulis sebagai lelucon. Postingan tersebut, yang ditujukan kepada Menteri Pertahanan Pete Hegseth, dimulai dengan argumen "Kita punya kanal dengan senjata nuklir." Postingan tersebut diakhiri dengan: Bos Anda adalah seorang pembangun. Trump tidak ingin bersikap baik dengan koalisi negara-negara yang dibencinya untuk berpatroli di Selat Hormuz. Dia ingin memotong pita dan menonton berita di Fox News. "KANAL TRUMP DIBUKA — TERBESAR DALAM SEJARAH MANUSIA." Tuan Menteri, berikan dia berita itu dan Anda memenangkan perang dan mempertahankan pekerjaan Anda. Kita bahkan bisa mengenakan tarif pada kapal tanker. DM saya terbuka. Sebuah pembaruan di bawah postingan tersebut juga membagikan tangkapan layar postingan X Gingrich dengan keterangan: "Newt menyetujui." Postingan tersebut tampaknya menanggapi pernyataan Hegseth selama konferensi pers bahwa Selat Hormuz telah dibuka, di mana ia mengatakan bahwa "satu-satunya hal yang menghalangi transit di selat saat ini adalah Iran yang menembaki kapal." Perang Iran menyebabkan penutupan efektif jalur pelayaran utama antara Teluk Persia dan Teluk Oman, yang mengakibatkan kenaikan harga minyak di seluruh dunia. Trump telah meminta negara-negara lain untuk mengirim kapal untuk membantu membuka blokade selat tersebut. Namun, banyak negara menolak permintaan tersebut dan enggan terlibat dalam perang dengan Iran. Snopes telah meliput banyak rumor tentang kenaikan harga minyak sebagai akibat dari Perang Iran.

claim: Donald Trump mencuit bahwa ia menyumbangkan masing-masing $500.000 untuk biaya dua deputi sheriff Los Angeles County yang ditembak dan terluka parah pada 12 September 2020.
evidence: Pemanasan global telah berulang kali terbukti sebagai kebohongan. http://t.co/RK1UoS8e Kaum kiri perlu sedikit realita.— Pemborosan! Dengan utang $16 triliun dan defisit anggaran $1 triliun, @BarackObama mengirimkan $770 juta ke luar negeri "untuk memerangi pemanasan global" http://t.co/op9x8BYl— Donald J.
