It also reported that 26 FBI informants also joined the crowd, four of whom entered the Capitol. Just the News linked to an "after-action report" it said it had obtained and also reported that 274 agents were in the crowd. The reports appeared to suggest that the long-standing rumor that undercover FBI agents had incited the violence was true. Two days later, on Sept. 27, President Donald Trump relayed the claim on Truth Social, in a post that has since been deleted (archived): (Truth Social user @realDonaldTrump) In the post, Trump called Jan. 6 a hoax. He also said these claims directly contradicted Wray's testimony. After Jan. 6, 2021, a House subcommittee on Jan. 6 began to conduct an investigation. This subcommittee disbanded in January 2023 after 18 months of work. However, in early September 2025 the House voted to open a new subcommittee chaired by Rep. Barry Loudermilk, a Republican from Georgia, to reinvestigate the matter, including the conclusions of the first subcommittee. The new subcommittee included Democrats. Snopes reviewed the after-action report published by Just the News, which did not support the claims in the outlet's story. We could not identify who The Blaze's anonymous source was, therefore we could not confirm its report. We have contacted both the FBI and the new House subcommittee and we will update this report should they respond. However, we found a Sept. 26, 2025, post on X (archived) by the new subcommittee on Jan. 6, which included the apparent screen capture of a spreadsheet: The row concerning the deployment of FBI agents during Jan. 6 read: Deployment date: 1/6/2021 Position type: Agents Event: 1/6 incident Field Office/HQ Division: WFO [Washington Field Office] Total Personnel Count: 274 Notes: This number includes agents that responded to the Capitol grounds as well as inside the Capitol, the pipe bombs, and the red truck that was believed to contain explosive devices as well as CDC/ADCS. While the number matched the reports by The Blaze and Just the News, the notes did not corroborate the claim that the FBI agents were undercover. While the exact number of FBI agents deployed had not been publicly reported before, the allegations that the FBI and its former director, Wray, had attempted to keep it secret did not hold up, either. For example, a December 2024 report by the Department of Justice's Office of the Inspector General acknowledged that in light of the intelligence the FBI was gathering on the Jan. 6 protest, "hundreds of highly trained specialized teams" would "be meeting DC." It falls under the FBI's responsibility to support a law enforcement response for crowd control should the circumstances call for it, as this DOJ report explained. However, the same report said the investigation had revealed no proof this had been the case. "We found no evidence in the materials we reviewed or the testimony we received showing or suggesting that the FBI had undercover employees in the various protest crowds, or at the Capitol, on January 6," the report said. Instead, the FBI deployed agents after the riot began. The report read: Indeed, after the Capitol was breached by rioters on January 6, the FBI was in a position to deploy tactical assets to help clear the Capitol of protesters and to help USCP secure the perimeter around the Capitol Complex. The FBI told Congress that its posture for January 6 preparations was "extraordinary," and we found that the FBI effectively carried out its tactical support function on January 6. Citing the DOJ report, The Blaze's story included one sentence that appeared to seed doubt. "Depending how one reads 'undercover' agents versus 'plainclothes agents,' both statements could be true," it read. It is important to note that most FBI agents do not wear uniforms in the field, unlike local law enforcement. Instead, most wear civilian clothes, except for tactical teams, who instead may wear tactical gear. As a rule, the dress code is business casual. The Blaze's and Just the News' reports did not cite "undercover" agents, but plainclothes agents, which is consistent with FBI standards for dress. In sum, despite suggestions by these two news outlets and the now-deleted post by Trump, there was no evidence that the FBI agents deployed on Jan. 6 were undercover. We could not independently confirm the number, though it is in line with the DOJ report published in December 2024.

claim: Amy Coney Barrett's nomination to the Supreme Court would be confirmed in October 2020, by a Senate majority that represents 15 million fewer people than the minority party.
evidence: After Kavanaugh's selection in 2018, Barrett was viewed as a possible nominee for a future U.S. Supreme Court vacancy.[107] After the death of Associate Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg on September 18, 2020, Barrett was widely mentioned as the front-runner to succeed her.[108] On September 26, 2020, Trump announced his intention to nominate Barrett to fill the vacancy created by Ginsburg's death.[7][109] Barrett's nomination was generally supported by Republicans, who sought to confirm her before the 2020 United States presidential election.[110] She was a favorite among the Christian right and social conservatives.[72][111][112] Democrats generally opposed the nomination, and were opposed to filling the court vacancy while election voting was already underway in many states.[110] Many observers were angered by the move to fill the vacancy only four months before the end of Trump's term, as the Senate Republican majority had refused to consider President Barack Obama's nomination of Merrick Garland in 2016, more than ten months before the end of his presidency.[110][113][114] In October, the American Bar Association rated Barrett "well qualified" for the Supreme Court opening, its highest rating.[115]

claim: Artificial intelligence data centers "drive up electric bills by 30 or 40%" and "suck up 500,000 gallons of water a day.”
evidence: Florida gubernatorial candidate James Fishback is a critic of artificial intelligence data centers. Fishback, who is competing against frontrunner U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds for the Republican nomination, says AI data centers offer few benefits for Floridians."What it actually does, is it drives up your electric bills by 30 or 40%. Anybody want to pay more on their electric bills? Or what it does is, it sucks up 500,000 gallons of water a day and then puts gray water back in the water supply," Fishback said during a Feb. 26 campaign stop in Pensacola. AI data centers are typically used to train artificial intelligence models, run AI responses and process massive datasets. Estimates show some facilities use at least 500,000 gallons of water a day to cool their equipment. AI data centers are also driving up consumers’ electricity rates. However, we found no public data showing they are responsible for 30% or 40% bill increases and Fishback didn’t respond to PolitiFact’s questions about his comments. Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, and state lawmakers from both political parties are pushing to regulate AI data centers. The Florida Senate unanimously passed a bill Feb. 26 with requirements related to moderating electricity rates and water management. Data centers house critical software systems and data in rows of computer servers, storage systems and networking equipment. Any time you send an email, watch a TikTok video or read an article online, you’re interacting with a data center. Data centers have existed since the mid 1940s, but their recent expansion to support AI technologies has created mega facilities that consume far more power than previous buildings. The newest AI data centers often span hundreds to over 1,000 acres, about the size of 750 American football fields. Meta’s planned Hyperion AI data center site in the farming community of Richland Parish, Louisiana, could be four times the size of Manhattan’s Central Park. Newer data centers can use as much electricity as a small city. In 2024, U.S. data centers consumed about 4% of the country’s total electricity. The International Energy Agency found that a typical AI data center consumes as much electricity as 100,000 homes annually, and that the largest ones under construction would likely consume 20 times as much. Without legislative protections, consumers could see their electric bills increase to cover data centers’ energy demands, experts said. Consumers often foot the bill for equipment and power grid upgrades that data centers require. "In general, data centers can raise power bills in Florida," said Ari Peskoe, director of Harvard Law School’s Electricity Law Initiative. Utility companies are spending "billions of dollars on new infrastructure to provide power to data centers," and utility rate structures allow these costs to be shared across the service territory, raising everyone’s bills. Experts said it’s difficult to estimate how much AI data centers are driving up consumers’ electric bills. Utilities and state regulatory commissions shield data center rates from public review, Peskoe said, making it difficult to measure the effect of new data center development. "How much the impact of large industrial loads on power capacity buildout and wholesale markets is eventually passed down to residential customers is largely confidential and is affected by many factors," said Shaolei Ren, a University of California, Riverside, professor who researches AI’s environmental footprint. PolitiFact found no news reports, government analyses or studies that corroborate Fishback’s figures of 30% to 40% electric bill increases. A few reports detail more modest rate increases because of data centers, and researchers project possible rate hikes in the future. A June 2025 study, for example, found that data centers are poised to raise electric bills by 8% nationwide by 2030, and by 25% in places such as Virginia, which has a higher concentration of these facilities. One 2024 study estimated that Virginia electric bills could increase up to 70% to meet data center demand. The New York Times reported in August 2025 that electric bills for a typical Ohio household increased at least $15 a month, which would be around an 11% increase based on back-of-the-envelope math, that summer because of data centers. CNBC reported in November 2025 that electric bills in states with large numbers of AI data centers have climbed much faster than the national average year-over-year. August 2025 prices increased 13% in Virginia, 16% in Illinois and 12% in Ohio compared with the previous year, the outlet found. The national average increase was 6.1%. Data centers require water to absorb heat from equipment, such as chips, servers and hard drives, to prevent overheating and maintain performance. Nearly all data centers require significant water consumption, sometimes stressing local resources. A typical mid-sized data center uses around the amount Fishback cited — 300,000 to 500,000 gallons per day — about as much as 1,000 U.S. households. A larger AI data center can use up to 5 million gallons of water a day, as much as a 50,000-person city. And, according to recent water agreements, some state-of-the-art data centers are using 8 million gallons per day. Approximately 80% of the water withdrawn for data centers evaporates. The remaining 20% is discharged to wastewater processing facilities, which must adhere to Clean Water Act guidelines for screening, treatment and disinfection. Technological advances, such as air cooling and immersion cooling, have helped reduce water use. Data centers with closed-loop cooling systems, for example, can reduce their freshwater use up to 70% "Generally, data centers are getting significantly more efficient than they were 10 years ago," said Ren. "On the other hand, the increasing scale of new data centers still drives up their total impacts," such as peak water demand during the hottest days of the year. A study Ren co-authored found that data centers’ water use creates significant stress on local water systems. Many communities lack the infrastructure to meet peak-day withdrawals, which can lead the facilities to shift to less efficient cooling practices and increase the burden on the power grid. If current trends continue, the study projected that by 2030, U.S. data centers will require 697 million to 1.451 billion gallons per day at peak capacity — comparable to the typical daily supply of New York City. Fishback said AI data centers "drive up electric bills by 30 or 40%" and "suck up 500,000 gallons of water a day." The facilities are raising consumers’ electric bills. Rate hike projections in states with high concentrations of data centers come close to Fishback’s estimate, but we found no public data showing the facilities are currently responsible for 30% or 40% bill increases. Data centers consume as much as, if not more, than 500,000 gallons a day. Some hyperscale AI facilities are estimated to use 5 million to 8 million gallons a day. Fishback’s statement is partially accurate but leaves out important details. We rate it Half True.

claim: Thirty percent of U.S. medical residents are international medical graduates, and 10,000 of 43,000 residency spots are filled by H-1B visa holders.
evidence: President Donald Trump announced that H-1B visa fees would increase to $100,000, a move that some social media users said could worsen U.S. physician shortages. As of Sept. 21, new H-1B visa applications will require a $100,000 fee, up from $2,000 to $5,000. The Trump administration said the fee is to prevent American workers from "being replaced with lower-paid foreign labor." The H-1B visa program lets employers temporarily hire foreign workers in specialty fields. Several medical associations sent a Sept. 25 letter to Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, asking for physicians and other medical personnel to be exempted from the new H-1B visa fee, because of a critical need for doctors as the U.S. population grows. While policy experts said the proclamation allows for exempting certain industries from the fee, the Trump administration hasn’t announced any exceptions yet. A doctor who also hosts a podcast posted data on X that he said showed how many medical residency spots are filled by H-1B visa holders. "This will be absolutely devastating in the medical field. ~30% residents are international medical graduates & ~10k of 43k residency spots are filled by docs with H-1B visas," the Sept. 19 post said. "Previously the h-1B fee was <$5,000. No hospital will pay a $100k fee for a $55k resident salary." Residency is the phase of physician training when recent medical school graduates participate in supervised clinical training in a healthcare facility. The X post is partially accurate but leaves out important details about international students and medical residents in the U.S. The X post cited as evidence a 2025 National Resident Matching Program report that says approximately 30% of U.S. medical residents are international medical graduates. It’s important to note, though, that this category includes foreign graduates and U.S. citizens who graduated from international schools. The report says that of international medical graduates who obtained a spot in first-year U.S. residency programs, 3,108 were U.S. citizens and 6,653 were noncitizens. It’s inaccurate that 10,000 of 43,000 resident spots are filled by H-1B visa holders. The X post cites a 2017 report that analyzed 2016 Labor Department data. This data shows how many working U.S. physicians were certified for H-1B visas. It includes all doctors, not only those in residency. (More recent Labor Department data from the last quarter of 2025, covering determinations from Oct. 1, 2024, through June 30, shows that around 9,000 physicians were certified for H-1B visas.) The American Medical Association says the most common visa used by medical graduates for medical residency is the J-1 visa, which is for graduate medical education or training, among other purposes. The majority of H1-B visa workers have been hired in STEM — science, technology, engineering and math — occupations, with about two-thirds working in computer-related fields, according to the Congressional Research Service. Nancy Nielsen, University at Buffalo senior associate dean for health policy, also confirmed to PolitiFact that the most common visa used for medical residency is the J-1 visa. She said for context, in her school, they have about 830 medical residents and only 27 are on H-1B visas. PolitiFact reached out to the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates, a division of Intealth, which helps medical graduates with sponsorship obtain training in the U.S., and the commission said to its knowledge, no centralized data repository exists for H-1B physicians in residency. The H-1B visa is available to graduates of foreign medical schools who have a license or other authorization required by the state where they’ll practice, and people who have an unrestricted license to practice medicine or have graduated from a U.S. medical school, according to the American Medical Association. Prospective H-1B employers must attest that they will pay the H-1B worker the greater of the actual wage paid to similar employees or the prevailing wages for that occupation. The visa status is generally valid for up to three years and renewable for another three years, but can be extended if the employer sponsors the worker for permanent residency. Congress caps the number of new H-1B visas at 85,000 per fiscal year, including 20,000 for noncitizens who earned a U.S. master’s degree or higher. The fiscal year 2025 cap was met in December 2024. Daniel Costa, director of immigration law and policy research at the Economic Policy Institute, a left-of-center think tank, told PolitiFact that the H-1B visa fee must be paid by the employer, not the employee. "The H-1B employees are generally not allowed to pay for the primary fees for visas and processing, they are the responsibility of the employer," Costa said via email Sept. 24. An X user said that 30% of medical residents are international medical graduates and 10,000 of 43,000 medical residency spots are filled by doctors with H-1B visas. Data shows that approximately 30% of medical residents are international medical graduates, which includes both U.S. citizens and noncitizens. Ten thousand doctors hold H-1B visas, but that represents all physicians, not only those in residency. The American Medical Association says the most common visa used by medical graduates for medical residency is the J-1 visa. The statement is partially accurate but leaves out important details. We rate it Half True.

claim: U.S. President Donald Trump, speaking at an event for fast food chain McDonald's, complained about restrictions on water that "comes from heaven" and said: "So you want to wash your hands? Or, like me, I want to wash my hair. I lather up. And … there's no water. … I won't mention the third item in the bathroom because I always get criticized. … If you don't know what I'm talking about then you shouldn't be owning a McDonald's franchise."
evidence: About this rating A rumor that U.S. President Donald Trump made a comment during a speech about washing hands, hair and "a third item in the bathroom" circulated online in mid-November 2025. Social media users on X (archived),Facebook, Instagram andThreads (archived) claimed that Trump spoke about restrictions on water that "comes from Heaven" and how such limitations impacted what can be done in the bathroom. Some users said the president followed the bathroom comment with: "If you don't know what I'm talking about then you shouldn't be owning a McDonald's franchise." For example, one X user quoted Trump as saying (archived): They had restrictions on water. It comes down from heaven, right? So you want to wash your hands, or like me, you want to wash your hair. I lather up. And there's no water. I won't mention the third item in the bathroom because I always get criticized. If you don't know what I'm talking about then you shouldn't be owning a McDonald's franchise. A post shared by @letsdiscusssomething In short, the quote was correctly attributed to Trump. The X user above cut out some of his words, seemingly for brevity, but did not change the essence of his remarks. The president was the keynote speaker for the 2025 McDonald's Impact Summit, an annual meeting of the fast food chain's franchise owners, suppliers and executives, on Nov. 17, and said the quote during his address. The entire speech was available to watch on theWhite House's official YouTube channel, with the quote in question starting at the 35:47 mark. The passage in its full context went as follows: Upon taking office, I signed an order requiring that for every new regulation, 10 old job-killing regulations have to be eliminated. So if we put in a new regulation, then we get rid of 10. Otherwise you don't get the new regulation. And you know, we got rid of the drip-drip water. We call it the drip-drip, where it drip-drips out of the sink. States with tremendous water, so much water they have nothing but problems getting rid — they had restrictions on water. It comes down from heaven, right? They had restrictions on water. So you want to wash your hands? Or, like me, I want to wash my hair. I lather up. And I turn that … there's no water. The water's, drip. They call it. They put a restrictor on. I won't mention the third item in the bathroom because I always get criticized. I used to mention that but I don't mention it anymore, because you know what I … I think you do. If you don't know what I'm talking about then you shouldn't be owning a McDonald's franchise. You wouldn't do well even with a McDonald's franchise. The White House said via email that Trump was referring to anexecutive order from April 9, 2025, that sought to undo "the left's war on water pressure" and "free" the public "from excessive regulations that turned a basic household item into a bureaucratic nightmare." The "third item in the bathroom" Trump referenced seemingly was the toilet. The White House declined to specify and instead said: "Off record — if you can't figure out the third item in a bathroom after showers and sinks, you're an idiot." (Snopes did not agree to speak off the record, perjournalistic standards, before this comment was made.) McDonald's did not respond to a request for comment as of this writing. We will update this article if we get a reply. Trump's remarks at the event were primarily about the economy, with digressions about water pressure, military strikes on Iran's nuclear facilities, the renaming of the Gulf of Mexico and his love of McDonald's products,Reuters reported.

claim: Balen Shah Is Nepal’s New Prime Minister
evidence: Following the recent violent protests in Nepal that led to the formation of an interim government, several social media posts are circulating claiming that Kathmandu Mayor Balendra Shah has been appointed as the new Prime Minister of the Himalayan nation.  The text with Balendra Shah's video reads: 'नेपाल के नये प्रधानमंत्री बने बालेन्द्र शाह'  (English translation: 'Balendra Shah becomes new prime minister of Nepal')  The above post can be seen here (archive)  FACT CHECK  NewsMobile fact-checked the claim and found it to be false.  A keyword search led us to multiple news reports on Nepal's interim government. According to credible reports, former Chief Justice Sushila Karki has been sworn in as the interim Prime Minister of Nepal. She took the oath late at night on September 12, 2025. (These reports can be seen here, here and here)

claim: In 2016, U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz of Texas, then a Republican presidential candidate, said, "We're liable to wake up one morning and Donald, if he were president, would have nuked Denmark."
evidence: About this rating In January 2026, a decade-old video (archived) circulated online of then-Republican presidential hopeful Ted Cruz speaking about Donald Trump at a campaign event in New Hampshire. According the video, the U.S. senator from Texas said, "We're liable to wake up one morning and Donald, if he were president, would have nuked Denmark." The alleged quote also circulated on Facebook (archived), Instagram (archived), Threads (archived), Bluesky (archived) and Reddit (archived). The clip circulated as tensions heightened over the Trump administration's stated desire to acquire Greenland, an island in the Arctic that is an autonomous territory of Denmark. At the time of this writing, the administration had not ruled out using military power to take the island, and Denmark and its European allies had reportedly increased their military presence in the region. Social media users who shared the quote correctly attributed it to Cruz. Cruz spoke the words when he answered a journalist's question during a press event in Goffstown, New Hampshire, on Feb. 3, 2016, according to C-SPAN (archived). Cruz said of his Trump's social media posts (at 4:21, emphasis ours): I don't know anyone who would be comfortable with someone who behaves this way having his finger on the button. I mean, we're liable to wake up one morning and Donald, if he were president, would have nuked Denmark. That's not the temperament of a leader to keep this country safe. Cruz was the first candidate to enter the Republican Party's primaries for the 2016 presidential election, positioning himself as a conservative alternative to Trump. Cruz dropped out of that race in early May 2016, around three months after making the remark about Trump nuking Denmark. Trump would go on to win the Republican nomination and the presidential election in 2016. Cruz later revised his opinion of Trump, including endorsing him in the Republican primaries leading into the 2024 U.S. presidential election that he would go on to win.

claim: “California memberikan $83,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang kita terima dari pemerintah federal. Texas? Menerima $71,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang mereka berikan kepada pemerintah federal. 71% dari PDB AS berasal dari daerah-daerah yang didominasi Partai Demokrat.”
evidence: Menjelang kemungkinan kampanye presiden pada tahun 2028, Gubernur California Gavin Newsom, seorang Demokrat, telah mencari dukungan lintas partai dari orang-orang yang mendengarkan podcast konservatif. Podcast terbaru yang diikuti Newsom, pada 14 Juli, adalah podcast Shawn Ryan. Dalam salah satu percakapan, Newsom menyoroti pentingnya California dalam perekonomian nasional. "California memberikan $83,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang kita terima dari pemerintah federal," kata Newsom dalam unggahan X pada 14 Juli yang menyoroti wawancara tersebut. "Texas? Menerima $71,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang mereka berikan kepada pemerintah federal. 71% dari PDB AS berasal dari daerah-daerah yang didominasi Partai Demokrat." California Crackup? Coba lagi. California memberikan $83,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang kita terima dari pemerintah federal. Texas? Menerima $71,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang mereka berikan kepada pemerintah federal. 71% dari PDB AS berasal dari daerah-daerah yang didominasi Partai Demokrat. pic.twitter.com/8yveQsdUJm Statistik Newsom tentang neraca pembayaran kepada pemerintah federal sebagian besar akurat. Tetapi angkanya untuk produk domestik bruto yang dihasilkan di daerah-daerah biru — mereka yang memilih Demokrat untuk presiden — sudah usang. Kantor Newsom tidak memberikan bukti yang mendukung klaimnya. Angka Newsom tentang neraca pembayaran sesuai dengan data dari Rockefeller Institute of Government, sebuah lembaga think tank nonpartisan yang berbasis di Albany, New York. Kelompok tersebut meneliti apa yang dibayarkan penduduk setiap negara bagian kepada pemerintah federal dalam bentuk pajak, dibandingkan dengan apa yang diterima negara bagian dan penduduknya dalam bentuk bantuan federal — mulai dari biaya infrastruktur hingga pembayaran jaring pengaman seperti Medicaid dan kupon makanan. Lembaga tersebut menemukan bahwa pada tahun 2022 — data terbaru yang tersedia — total pembayaran California kepada pemerintah federal melebihi distribusinya dari pemerintah federal sebesar $83,1 miliar, sementara uang federal yang masuk ke Texas melebihi pembayarannya kepada pemerintah federal sebesar $71,1 miliar. Itu sejalan dengan apa yang dikatakan Newsom. Angka tersebut termasuk pengeluaran terkait virus corona. Lembaga tersebut juga memberikan angka tanpa memperhitungkan pengeluaran terkait virus corona; angkanya berbeda, tetapi menunjukkan pola umum yang sama. Dengan menggunakan angka non-virus corona, California mengirimkan $126,5 miliar lebih banyak ke pemerintah federal daripada yang diterimanya, sementara Texas menerima $44,6 miliar lebih banyak dari pemerintah federal daripada yang dikirimnya. California dan Texas adalah negara bagian besar dengan sektor bisnis yang makmur dan sejumlah besar penduduk berpenghasilan rendah. Jadi mengapa neraca pembayaran mereka dengan pemerintah federal sangat berbeda? Joel Tirado, direktur komunikasi untuk Rockefeller Institute, mengatakan perbedaan utama berasal dari pajak yang dibayarkan. "Warga California umumnya memberikan kontribusi lebih banyak dalam pajak penghasilan individu dan perusahaan," kata Tirado. "Ini mencerminkan pendapatan per kapita yang tinggi di banyak bagian California, serta basis pajak perusahaan California yang besar. Dan karena California memiliki sekitar 30 persen lebih banyak penduduk daripada Texas, perbedaan tersebut menjadi semakin besar." Mengenai pertanyaan produk domestik bruto untuk negara bagian yang didominasi Partai Demokrat, Newsom mengutip angka yang berlaku untuk tahun 2020, tetapi tidak untuk tahun 2024. Statistik ini berasal dari perhitungan Brookings Institution, sebuah lembaga think tank nonpartisan. Pada tahun 2020, Joe Biden memenangkan daerah-daerah yang secara kolektif menghasilkan 71% dari PDB nasional — ukuran luas aktivitas ekonomi — sementara Presiden Donald Trump memenangkan daerah-daerah yang menyumbang 29% dari PDB. Empat tahun kemudian, ketika Trump mengalahkan Wakil Presiden saat itu, Kamala Harris, selisihnya lebih kecil. Harris memenangkan daerah-daerah yang mewakili 62% dari PDB nasional, sementara Trump memenangkan daerah-daerah yang mewakili 38% dari PDB. Peningkatan persentase Trump sebagian berasal dari kemenangannya di beberapa daerah besar dan dinamis secara ekonomi yang dimenangkan Biden pada tahun 2020, termasuk Maricopa County, Arizona (Phoenix), Miami-Dade County, Florida (Miami), Tarrant County, Texas (Fort Worth), Duval County, Florida (Jacksonville) dan Pinellas County, Florida (St. Petersburg). Newsom mengatakan, "California memberikan $83,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang kita terima dari pemerintah federal. Texas? Menerima $71,1 miliar lebih banyak daripada yang mereka berikan kepada pemerintah federal. 71% dari PDB AS berasal dari daerah-daerah yang didominasi Partai Demokrat." Mengenai neraca pembayaran kepada pemerintah federal, Newsom benar untuk kedua negara bagian tersebut jika pengeluaran terkait virus corona disertakan, dan menggunakan data tahun 2022, yang merupakan data terbaru yang tersedia. Kesenjangan antara kedua negara bagian tersebut serupa jika pengeluaran terkait virus corona dikecualikan. Mengenai pangsa PDB nasional dari daerah-daerah yang didominasi Partai Demokrat, data Newsom sudah usang. Angka 71% benar untuk tahun 2020, tetapi menyusut menjadi 62% untuk tahun 2024 karena Trump berhasil merebut beberapa daerah yang padat penduduk dan memiliki potensi ekonomi yang besar. Kami menilai pernyataan tersebut Sebagian Besar Benar.

claim: Panglima Angkatan Darat India, Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi, mengakui bahwa 250 tentara gugur di sepanjang Garis Kontrol (LOC) selama konflik baru-baru ini dengan Pakistan.
evidence: Sebuah video yang diduga menunjukkan Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi, Kepala Staf Angkatan Darat (COAS), mengakui bahwa 250 tentara India gugur di sepanjang Garis Kontrol (LOC) selama konflik baru-baru ini dengan Pakistan telah muncul di platform media sosial. Newschecker menyelidiki rekaman viral tersebut, dan menemukan bahwa itu telah dimanipulasi. Dalam rekaman viral yang berdurasi lebih dari dua menit itu, Jenderal Dwivedi diduga terdengar mengatakan, "Angkatan Darat India menderita kerugian besar di sepanjang LOC, dengan 250 tentara gugur. Pergerakan aset kami juga dipantau secara ketat oleh satelit Pakistan dan Tiongkok." Cuplikan layar dari unggahan X oleh @InsiderWB. Unggahan serupa dapat dilihat di sini, di sini, dan di sini. Baca Juga: Apakah Maladewa Menampilkan Gambar PM Modi di Gedung Kementerian Pertahanan dengan Kata 'Menyerah'? Pemeriksaan Fakta/Verifikasi. Pencarian kata kunci untuk "Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi," "250 tentara," dan "Pakistan" di Google tidak menghasilkan laporan/rilis kredibel apa pun tentang dugaan pengakuan kerugian oleh Kepala Angkatan Darat. Kami memperhatikan tanda air dari media berita 'The Indian Express' di pojok kanan atas rekaman viral tersebut. Cuplikan layar dari video viral. Berdasarkan petunjuk tersebut, kami mencari kata kunci "The Indian Express," "Kepala Angkatan Darat," dan "Operasi Sindoor" di YouTube yang membawa kami ke sebuah laporan video, tertanggal 26 Juli 2025, yang menunjukkan Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi dengan latar belakang yang sama seperti yang terlihat dalam klip viral tersebut. "Pada Hari Kemenangan Kargil 2025, Kepala Angkatan Darat Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi menyampaikan pesan yang kuat kepada Pakistan, menyatakan bahwa serangan bedah Operasi Sindoor adalah peringatan yang jelas bahwa terorisme dan para pendukungnya tidak akan dibiarkan begitu saja. Berbicara di Monumen Perang Kargil, Jenderal Dwivedi menyebut serangan itu sebagai tanggapan langsung terhadap serangan teror Pahalgam, yang ia gambarkan sebagai luka nasional yang mendalam…," demikian deskripsinya. Sekitar menit ke-1:13 dalam video tersebut, Kepala Staf Angkatan Darat terdengar mengatakan, "Pada tahun 1999, Angkatan Darat India meraih kemenangan yang tak tertandingi di puncak Kargil dalam Operasi Vijay. Selama perang, Angkatan Darat India mengusir tentara dan penyusup Pakistan yang menduduki puncak-puncak tinggi, mengusir mereka dari perbatasan, dan mengibarkan bendera tiga warna di puncak-puncak yang tertutup salju ini… India menegaskan bahwa tidak ada niat immoral yang akan berhasil dalam batas wilayahnya. Persatuan dan integritas India tidak akan dikompromikan." Cuplikan layar dari video YouTube oleh The Indian Express: "Meneruskan tradisi itu, selama Operasi Sindoor, Angkatan Darat India, dengan keberanian dan tekad yang sama, menargetkan infrastruktur teroris yang didukung Pakistan dan menggagalkan aktivitas agresif Pakistan lainnya, mencapai kemenangan yang menentukan. Kami memberi kesempatan pada perdamaian tetapi dalam tanggapannya, mereka (Pakistan) menunjukkan sikap pengecut… Operasi Sindoor adalah tekad, pesan, dan tanggapan kami. Serangan teror pengecut di Pahalgam adalah luka bagi seluruh bangsa. India tidak hanya menyatakan duka cita kali ini, tetapi memutuskan bahwa tanggapannya akan menentukan…" Bagian pidatonya ini tidak memuat detail tentang korban jiwa dalam konflik tersebut, seperti yang terlihat dalam klip viral, yang menunjukkan bahwa rekaman tersebut dapat dimanipulasi. Pidato Jenderal Dwivedi pada Hari Kemenangan Kargil 2025 disiarkan langsung di saluran YouTube The Hindu pada 26 Juli 2025. Segmen yang disebutkan di atas dapat dilihat pada menit ke 3:55 dalam video tersebut. Siaran langsung ini juga tidak menunjukkan dia mengakui kematian 250 tentara, sehingga mengkonfirmasi bahwa pernyataan palsu tersebut disisipkan dalam pidato Kepala Angkatan Darat untuk membuat klaim palsu. Cuplikan layar dari video YouTube oleh The Hindu. Sebuah rilis dari Kementerian Pertahanan pada peringatan ke-26 Kargil Vijay Diwas memuat poin-poin penting dari pidato Jenderal Dwivedi, yang menyatakan bahwa ia "memberikan penghormatan tulus kepada para prajurit yang gugur dalam Perang Kargil, memuji keberanian dan pengorbanan mereka yang tak tergoyahkan. Ia merenungkan kemenangan bersejarah Angkatan Darat India pada tahun 1999 dan pembelaan teguh kedaulatan nasional selama Operasi Sindoor baru-baru ini…" Rilis tersebut juga tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang dugaan pengakuan korban oleh Kepala Angkatan Darat. Baca Juga: Ashwin Mengecam BCCI Terkait Pertandingan India Vs Pakistan di Piala Asia? Tidak, Kutipan Viral Itu Rekayasa. Menganalisis Klip Viral: Ketidaksesuaian Terdeteksi. Setelah menganalisis video viral dengan cermat, kami menemukan perubahan yang mencolok pada audio dari segmen 0:55-1:09 menit, di mana pernyataan yang diduga menyebutkan "250 tentara" terdengar. Selain itu, gerakan bibirnya di bagian ini tampak sedikit tidak alami. Lebih lanjut, sementara bingkai kacamata tanpa bingkainya sedikit terlihat dalam video oleh The Indian Express, bingkai tersebut sepenuhnya menyatu dengan wajahnya dalam rekaman viral. (Kiri-Kanan) Tangkapan layar dari video viral dan tangkapan layar dari video YouTube oleh The Indian Express. Kami menjalankan cuplikan audio yang membuat klaim viral tersebut pada beberapa alat deteksi AI, yang sebagian besar tidak menghasilkan hasil yang meyakinkan. Namun, Resemble.ai, alat deteksi audio AI, menyebut suara tersebut sebagai "palsu." Newschecker juga memeriksa segmen video viral, di mana Kepala Angkatan Darat diduga membuat klaim tersebut, di Deepware, yang mendeteksi keberadaan "deepfake" dalam rekaman tersebut. Cuplikan layar dari situs web Deepware: Tidak Ada Pernyataan Seperti Itu dari Kepala Staf Angkatan Darat: PIB. Lembaga Pemeriksa Fakta PIB pemerintah juga menyoroti "video manipulasi yang beredar online, yang secara keliru menunjukkan Kepala Staf Angkatan Darat India Upendra Dwivedi mengakui kehilangan lebih dari 250 tentara dalam perang melawan Pakistan." Menyebutnya sebagai "deepfake yang dihasilkan AI," PIB menegaskan, "Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi TIDAK pernah membuat pernyataan seperti itu." Cuplikan layar dari unggahan X oleh Pemeriksa Fakta PIB. Audio Palsu yang Disisipkan: Modus Operandi yang Berulang Terbantah. Para pejabat Pertahanan India telah menjadi target penyebar informasi yang salah sejak awal Operasi Sindoor, setelah serangan Pahalgam pada April 2025. Newschecker telah membantah beberapa klaim yang tidak akurat tentang Angkatan Bersenjata India - termasuk visual lama dan tidak terkait yang dibagikan untuk menunjukkan kerusakan pada peralatan militer, video potongan pejabat Pertahanan yang disebarkan untuk mendorong narasi pro-Pakistan, dan lain-lain. Baru-baru ini muncul tren baru di mana audio palsu/buatan AI disisipkan dalam pidato-pidato sebenarnya oleh para pejabat/menteri pertahanan tinggi, secara keliru mengaitkan pernyataan-pernyataan tersebut, tentang kerusakan aset pertahanan India, kepada mereka. Newschecker telah membongkar video-video CDS Jenderal Anil Chauhan, Wakil Kepala Staf Angkatan Darat Letnan Jenderal Rahul R Singh, Menteri Luar Negeri S Jaishankar, yang dimanipulasi menggunakan teknik yang sama. Baca Juga: Keluarga Muslim Dibantai oleh Anggota Bajrang Dal di UP? Tidak, Inilah Kebenaran di Balik Klip Viral Kesimpulan Oleh karena itu, kami menemukan bahwa video viral yang mengklaim menunjukkan Kepala Staf Angkatan Darat Jenderal Upendra Dwivedi mengakui bahwa 250 tentara India gugur selama konflik baru-baru ini dengan Pakistan, adalah hasil manipulasi. Sumber: Video YouTube oleh The Indian Express, Tanggal 26 Juli 2025; Video YouTube oleh The Hindu, Tanggal 26 Juli 2025; Siaran Pers oleh Kementerian Pertahanan, Tanggal 26 Juli 2025; X Post oleh PIB Fact Check, Tanggal 29 Juli 2025; Situs Web Resemble.ai; Situs Web Deepware.

claim: Hardik Pandya Meninggal Dunia Jelang Piala Asia 2025
evidence: Beberapa hari setelah BCCI mengumumkan skuad India untuk Piala Asia 2025 mendatang, sebuah klaim mengejutkan muncul di media sosial yang menyatakan bahwa pemain kriket Hardik Pandya telah meninggal dunia. Klaim tersebut berbunyi: ""हार्दिक पांड्या ने का हुआ निधन एशिया कप 2025 से पहले टीम इंडिया में पसरा मातम, tentu saja खिलाड़ी का "Hardik Pandya meninggal dunia, Tim India berduka sebelum Piala Asia 2025, pemain bintang meninggal dunia." Postingan di atas dapat dilihat di sini (arsip). PEMERIKSAAN FAKTA NewsMobile telah memeriksa fakta klaim viral tersebut dan menemukan bahwa klaim tersebut menyesatkan. Pencarian kata kunci tidak mengungkapkan laporan kredibel tentang kematian Pandya. Mengingat kedudukannya di kriket internasional, berita seperti itu pasti akan dilaporkan secara luas oleh media arus utama India dan internasional. Kami juga meninjau akun media sosial resmi Pandya dan tidak menemukan pengumuman seperti itu. Baik BCCI maupun keluarganya tidak mengeluarkan pernyataan apa pun yang mengkonfirmasi klaim tersebut. Kesimpulannya, klaim viral tentang kematian Hardik Pandya adalah salah. claim: Kongres menghapus Bagian 9 dan 10 dari Pasal 1 Konstitusi dari situs webnya.
evidence: BERITA TERBARU: periksa pembaruan di bawah ini. Apakah bagian-bagian Konstitusi AS yang berkaitan dengan habeas corpus dan kekuasaan Kongres menghilang dari situs web Kongres AS sebelum 6 Agustus 2025? Ya, itu benar: Versi arsip halaman tersebut mencatat perubahan yang menghilangkan bagian 9 dan 10 dari Pasal 1. Perpustakaan Kongres, yang mengelola situs web Kongres, mengakui penghapusan tersebut dan mengatakan bahwa itu disebabkan oleh "kesalahan pengkodean" yang akan ditangani. Kesalahan situs web tersebut tidak berdampak pada interpretasi atau penegakan Konstitusi. Klaim tersebut muncul dalam sebuah unggahan (diarsipkan di sini) yang diterbitkan di X pada 6 Agustus 2025. Unggahan tersebut dimulai dengan: BERITA TERBARU: Pemerintah AS baru saja menghapus bagian 9 dan 10 dari Pasal I Konstitusi AS dari situs web mereka. Unggahan tersebut melanjutkan: Perhatikan bahwa bagian 9 dan 10 berkaitan dengan: - Surat Perintah Habeas Corpus - Kemampuan Kongres untuk mengendalikan tarif. Beginilah tampilan unggahan di X pada saat penulisan: (Sumber: tangkapan layar unggahan oleh krassenstein di X.com) Video dari unggahan tersebut menunjukkan nama situs web: constitution.congress.gov, juga dikenal sebagai bagian Anotasi Konstitusi dari situs web Kongres AS, sumber resmi untuk informasi kongres. Kongres tidak mengelola situs web tersebut. Halaman Tentangnya (diarsipkan di sini) berbunyi: Congress.gov dikembangkan dan dipelihara oleh Perpustakaan Kongres menggunakan data yang berasal dan dimiliki oleh Senat Amerika Serikat dan Dewan Perwakilan Rakyat Amerika Serikat. Beginilah tampilannya pada saat penulisan: (Sumber: tangkapan layar constitution.congress.gov) Seperti yang terlihat pada tangkapan layar di atas, dua catatan ditambahkan ke halaman tersebut. Salah satunya menyatakan bahwa situs web Anotasi Konstitusi mengalami "masalah data". Catatan lain menjelaskan bahwa informasi yang hilang dihapus karena kesalahan teknis dan diharapkan akan dipulihkan: Karena kesalahan pengkodean, beberapa bagian Pasal 1 hilang di situs web Anotasi Konstitusi. Kami menyadari masalah ini dan sedang berupaya memperbaikinya. Kami berharap masalah ini akan segera terselesaikan. Perpustakaan Kongres mengkonfirmasi hal ini dalam unggahannya di X (diarsipkan di sini) yang diterbitkan pada 6 Agustus 2025. Lead Stories menerima pernyataan melalui email dari juru bicara Perpustakaan Kongres, Bill Ryan, setelah kami pertama kali menerbitkan artikel ini: Konstitusi Online yang Dianotasi adalah alat pendidikan yang mencakup diskusi tentang pendapat terbaru Mahkamah Agung yang terkait dengan teks konstitusi. Saat memperbarui situs untuk mencerminkan analisis para ahli konstitusi kami tentang dampak kasus-kasus terbaru pada Pasal I, Bagian 8-10, tim secara tidak sengaja menghapus tag XML. Hal ini mencegah publikasi semua isi Pasal I setelah bagian tengah Bagian 8. Masalah ini telah diperbaiki dan analisis konstitusional kami yang diperbarui sekarang tersedia. Kami sedang mengambil langkah-langkah untuk mencegah terulangnya hal ini di masa mendatang. Tautan ini menunjukkan isi halaman yang diawetkan dengan Konstitusi AS yang telah dianotasi, seperti yang terlihat pada tanggal 6 Agustus 2025. Menurut Wayback Machine, konten yang dihapus mencakup sebagian besar Bagian 8 dan seluruh Bagian 9 dan 10. (Sumber: tangkapan layar situs web Konstitusi yang telah dianotasi di archive.org) (klik gambar untuk melihat lebih besar.) Versi halaman lain yang tersimpan menunjukkan bahwa apa yang digambarkan sebagai "kesalahan pengkodean" tampaknya terjadi pada suatu waktu antara 17 Juli 2025 dan 6 Agustus 2025, dengan seluruh Konstitusi AS tidak tersedia pada tanggal 21 Juli 2025. Bagian 9 (diarsipkan di sini) melarang Kongres untuk melarang impor budak ke Amerika Serikat hingga tahun 1808 atau menangguhkan hak habeas corpus kecuali terjadi pemberontakan atau invasi. Bagian 10 (diarsipkan di sini) memberlakukan batasan pada negara bagian seperti membuat perjanjian dengan negara lain dan mengenakan tarif pada impor atau ekspor. Perubahan pada situs web kongres tidak memiliki kekuatan hukum. Konstitusi AS diratifikasi pada tahun 1789, lebih dari 200 tahun sebelum situs web tersebut dibuat.

claim: Cek Fakta: Keringanan pajak perjanjian perdagangan tidak berlaku untuk semua pekerja India di Inggris, bertentangan dengan klaim daring.
evidence: Kesepakatan perdagangan baru akan membebaskan sementara warga India yang dikirim ke Inggris oleh majikan mereka yang berbasis di India dari pembayaran iuran jaminan sosial Inggris, bukan membebaskan semua pekerja India di Inggris dari pajak tersebut, bertentangan dengan klaim daring. Inggris dan India mencapai kesepakatan perdagangan bebas yang akan mengurangi tarif untuk barang-barang termasuk mobil, makanan, dan wiski. Perjanjian tersebut, yang diumumkan pada 6 April, akan mencakup keringanan pajak bagi pekerja India yang dikirim ke Inggris untuk sementara dan majikan mereka. Gambar Reuters “Jadi kesepakatan perdagangan baru dengan India berarti bahwa migran India yang bekerja di Inggris tidak perlu membayar NI hingga 3 tahun? Bagaimana itu adil?” Sebuah unggahan pada 6 Mei di Facebook, yang merujuk pada pajak jaminan sosial, disebut asuransi nasional, menyatakan hal tersebut. Unggahan lain pada 12 Mei menyarankan bahwa pengecualian tersebut mencakup pajak selain jaminan sosial, serta akses gratis ke Layanan Kesehatan Nasional. Namun, keringanan pajak ini hanya akan berlaku untuk pekerja India yang dikirim oleh perusahaan mereka yang berbasis di India untuk bekerja di Inggris hingga tiga tahun, kata juru bicara Departemen Bisnis dan Perdagangan Inggris dalam sebuah email kepada Reuters. Keringanan pajak ini - yang disebut konvensi kontribusi ganda - berarti pemberi kerja dan karyawan tidak akan membayar asuransi nasional Inggris. Tetapi mereka akan terus membayarnya di India. Kesepakatan perdagangan berarti mereka tidak akan bertanggung jawab atas hal itu di kedua negara, tambah juru bicara tersebut. Hal ini juga berlaku untuk pekerja Inggris yang dipekerjakan oleh perusahaan Inggris di India. Mereka yang mendapat manfaat dari pengecualian biaya jaminan sosial harus membayar biaya tambahan kesehatan NHS, dan tidak akan dapat mengakses tunjangan Inggris, menurut sebuah makalah kebijakan pemerintah Inggris tentang kesepakatan tersebut, yang diterbitkan pada 6 Mei. Kementerian luar negeri India tidak menanggapi permintaan komentar. KESIMPULAN: Konteks hilang. Keringanan pajak tidak membebaskan semua pekerja India di Inggris dari pembayaran asuransi nasional atau biaya tambahan NHS, hanya warga India yang dikirim ke Inggris sementara oleh majikan mereka yang berbasis di India. Artikel ini diproduksi oleh tim Pemeriksaan Fakta Reuters. Baca lebih lanjut tentang pekerjaan pemeriksaan fakta kami. Standar Kami: Prinsip Kepercayaan Thomson Reuters. Bagikan:

Unggahan palsu Trump tentang penggunaan narkoba Musk menyebar di tengah perseteruan

Diterbitkan pada 6 Juni 2025 pukul 21:34

2 menit baca

Oleh

Bill McCarthy,

AFP AS

Ketika perselisihan antara Donald Trump dan penasihat miliardernya Elon Musk meledak menjadi pertengkaran publik yang sengit dan terkadang bersifat pribadi pada 5 Juni 2025, pengguna media sosial menyebarkan tangkapan layar yang tampaknya menunjukkan presiden AS menulis bahwa taipan Tesla dan SpaceX itu "terus-menerus mabuk karena ketamin." Tetapi unggahan Truth Social yang diduga itu palsu, Gedung Putih mengkonfirmasi kepada AFP, dan tidak muncul di unggahan Trump.

"Elon Musk atau 'Muskus yang Biasa-Biasa Saja' selalu mabuk ketamin. Selalu membuat janji yang tidak bisa ditepati. TIDAK BISA DIPERCAYA! Dia ingin pergi ke Mars, bagaimana kalau kamu kembali ke Afrika," demikian bunyi unggahan yang diduga berasal dari Trump, dengan cap waktu 5 Juni 2025 pukul 18.07 (2207 GMT).

Gambar

Tangkapan layar dari X yang diambil pada 6 Juni 2025

Gambar tersebut menyebar dengan cepat di berbagai platform termasuk X, Facebook, Instagram, dan Threads ketika pemimpin AS dan orang terkaya di dunia saling melontarkan sindiran dalam apa yang dimulai sebagai perselisihan mengenai RUU pengeluaran "besar dan indah" di hadapan Kongres. Keduanya saling menghina secara online setelah Trump menyatakan kekecewaannya atas kritik donor utamanya terhadap undang-undang tersebut, dengan Trump mengancam akan mencabut kontrak pemerintah besar Musk dan Musk mengklaim keberhasilan atas kemenangan pemilu Republikan pada tahun 2024. Pada suatu waktu, Musk menuduh bahwa presiden disebut-sebut dalam dokumen pemerintah mengenai Jeffrey Epstein, seorang pelaku kejahatan keuangan yang meninggal karena bunuh diri di penjara saat menunggu persidangan atas kejahatan seksual. Dampak buruknya muncul beberapa hari setelah New York Times melaporkan bahwa Musk menggunakan begitu banyak anestesi kuat ketamin saat kampanye 2024 sehingga ia mengalami masalah kandung kemih — tuduhan yang telah dibantah Musk. Namun, peninjauan versi langsung dan arsip dari unggahan Truth Social Trump mengungkapkan bahwa ia tidak menyebutkan ketamin dalam serangannya terhadap Musk. "Itu palsu," kata Gedung Putih tentang tangkapan layar yang diduga beredar online, dalam email tanggal 6 Juni 2025 kepada AFP. Presiden tidak mengunggah apa pun selama pukul 18.00 (2200 GMT) pada tanggal 5 Juni. Dua jam sebelumnya, ia mendukung rancangan undang-undangnya dan menulis: "Saya tidak keberatan Elon berbalik melawan saya, tetapi seharusnya dia melakukannya beberapa bulan yang lalu" (diarsipkan di sini). Unggahan berikutnya, setelah pukul 22.30 (0230 GMT), membagikan tautan ke artikel Newsmax tentang peringkat persetujuannya (diarsipkan di sini). Pencarian kata kunci tidak menghasilkan artikel berita kredibel yang merujuk pada unggahan yang diduga tersebut. AFP telah membantah beberapa unggahan Truth palsu. Unggahan media sosial yang secara keliru dikaitkan dengan Trump. Baca lebih lanjut laporan AFP tentang misinformasi mengenai politik AS di sini.
